.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_merge_review_strongest_restriction.md:2:title: "DLR-028 -- Fan-out merge: review strongest-restriction"
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_merge_review_strongest_restriction.md:3:description: "Validate mergeReviewRegistries strongest-restriction: clean A + P0 B → FAIL; duplicate findingId escalates to highest severity; non-active findings excluded; P1-only → CONDITIONAL; all clean → PASS."
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_merge_review_strongest_restriction.md:7:# DLR-028 -- Fan-out merge: review strongest-restriction
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_merge_review_strongest_restriction.md:15:Validates the review-specific strongest-restriction merge logic in `fanout-merge.cjs`.
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_merge_review_strongest_restriction.md:19:The strongest-restriction policy is what makes fan-out useful for review: if ANY lineage
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_merge_review_strongest_restriction.md:27:- Objective: Confirm `mergeReviewRegistries` applies strongest-restriction: any active P0 across any lineage → `mergedVerdict=FAIL`; duplicate findingId escalates to highest severity; non-active findings excluded; all clean → PASS; P1-only → CONDITIONAL.
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_merge_review_strongest_restriction.md:28:- Layer partition: review merge.
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_merge_review_strongest_restriction.md:29:- Real user request: `Validate the review fan-out strongest-restriction merge and confirm all 5 review unit tests pass, covering every verdict combination and the duplicate escalation rule.`
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_merge_review_strongest_restriction.md:30:- Expected signals: clean+P0 → `mergedVerdict=FAIL`, `activeP0=1`; all clean → `mergedVerdict=PASS`, `activeP0=0`; P1-only → `mergedVerdict=CONDITIONAL`; duplicate `findingId` P2+P0 → escalates to P0 → FAIL, single deduplicated finding; `resolved_false_positive` excluded from `activeP0`.
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_merge_review_strongest_restriction.md:40:- `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` present.
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_merge_review_strongest_restriction.md:44:1. Inspect `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` `mergeReviewRegistries` — confirm `SEVERITY_RANK` map (`{P0:3, P1:2, P2:1}`), `status !== 'active'` guard, `mergedVerdict` derivation formula.
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_merge_review_strongest_restriction.md:45:2. `bash: cd .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server && npx vitest run ../../runtime//tests/unit/fanout-merge.vitest.ts --reporter=verbose`
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_merge_review_strongest_restriction.md:56:- `mergedVerdict` logic inverted: P1-only produces FAIL instead of CONDITIONAL.
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_merge_review_strongest_restriction.md:66:| `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` | `mergeReviewRegistries`, `SEVERITY_RANK`, `mergedVerdict` derivation |
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_merge_review_strongest_restriction.md:72:| `tests/unit/fanout-merge.vitest.ts` | 5 review strongest-restriction tests (of 10 total) |

   132	
   133	function contentSortKey(record) {
   134	  const durableText = [
   135	    record.title,
   136	    record.summary,
   137	    record.description,
   138	    record.finding,
   139	    record.question,
   140	    record.direction,
   141	    record.severity,
   142	    record.status,
   143	  ].map(normalizeSortText).filter(Boolean).join('\u0001');
   144	  return durableText || stableStringify({ ...record, _lineages: undefined });
   145	}
   146	
   147	function contentIdentityKey(record) {
   148	  const durableText = [
   149	    record.title,
   150	    record.summary,
   151	    record.description,
   152	    record.finding,
   153	    record.question,
   154	    record.direction,
   155	  ].map(normalizeSortText).filter(Boolean).join('\u0001');
   156	  return durableText || stableStringify({
   157	    ...record,
   158	    _conflictOf: undefined,
   159	    _conflict_id: undefined,
   160	    _conflicts: undefined,
   161	    _lineages: undefined,
   162	    severity: undefined,
   163	    status: undefined,
   164	  });
   165	}
   166	
   167	function nearDuplicateContentKey(record) {
   168	  const durableText = [
   169	    record.summary,
   170	    record.description,
   230	function compareByContentThenId(left, right, idKeys) {
   231	  const leftContent = contentSortKey(left);
   232	  const rightContent = contentSortKey(right);
   233	  if (leftContent < rightContent) return -1;
   234	  if (leftContent > rightContent) return 1;
   235	
   236	  const leftId = normalizeSortText(idKeys.map((key) => left[key]).find(Boolean));
   237	  const rightId = normalizeSortText(idKeys.map((key) => right[key]).find(Boolean));
   238	  if (leftId < rightId) return -1;
   239	  if (leftId > rightId) return 1;
   240	
   241	  const leftFull = stableStringify(left);
   242	  const rightFull = stableStringify(right);
   243	  if (leftFull < rightFull) return -1;
   244	  if (leftFull > rightFull) return 1;
   245	  return 0;
   246	}
   247	
   248	function sortByContentThenId(records, idKeys) {
   249	  return [...records].sort((left, right) => compareByContentThenId(left, right, idKeys));
   250	}
   251	
   252	function addLineage(existing, label) {
   253	  if (!existing._lineages) existing._lineages = [];
   254	  if (!existing._lineages.includes(label)) existing._lineages.push(label);
   255	  existing._lineages.sort();
   256	}
   257	
   258	function mergeLineageLabels(existing, incoming, label) {
   259	  const lineages = new Set([...(existing._lineages || []), ...(incoming._lineages || []), label].filter(Boolean));
   260	  return [...lineages].sort();
   261	}
   262	
   263	function comparableRecord(record) {
   264	  const copy = { ...record };
   265	  delete copy._conflictOf;
   266	  delete copy._conflict_id;
   267	  delete copy._conflicts;
   268	  delete copy._lineages;
   269	  return copy;
   270	}
   271	
   272	function replaceRecord(target, source, lineages) {
   273	  const next = { ...source, _lineages: lineages };
   274	  for (const key of Object.keys(target)) {
   275	    delete target[key];
   276	  }
   277	  Object.assign(target, next);
   278	}
   279	
   280	function chooseCanonicalRecord(existing, incoming, idKeys) {
   281	  return compareByContentThenId(comparableRecord(incoming), comparableRecord(existing), idKeys) < 0
   282	    ? incoming
   283	    : existing;
   284	}
   285	
   286	function chooseReviewCanonicalRecord(existing, incoming, idKeys) {
   287	  const incomingRank = SEVERITY_RANK[incoming.severity] ?? 0;
   288	  const existingRank = SEVERITY_RANK[existing.severity] ?? 0;
   289	  if (incomingRank > existingRank) return incoming;
   290	  if (incomingRank < existingRank) return existing;
   291	  return chooseCanonicalRecord(existing, incoming, idKeys);
   292	}
   293	
   294	function conflictSafeRecord(record, baseId, idKey) {
   295	  const conflictId = `${baseId}--${contentDigest(record)}`;
   296	  return {
   297	    ...record,
   298	    [idKey]: conflictId,
   299	    _conflictOf: baseId,
   300	    _conflict_id: conflictId,
   301	  };
   302	}
   303	
   304	function attachConflictMarkers(records, baseId, idKey) {
   305	  if (records.length < 2) return records;
   306	  return records.map((record) => ({
   307	    ...record,
   308	    _conflicts: records
   309	      .filter((other) => other !== record)
   310	      .map((other) => ({
   311	        relation: 'CONTRADICTS',
   312	        originalId: baseId,
   313	        peerId: other[idKey],
   314	        peerLineages: other._lineages || [],
   315	        basis: 'same-id-different-content',
   316	      })),
   317	  }));
   318	}

   320	function parseBooleanOption(value) {
   521	 * Merge research findings registries from all lineages.
   522	 * Deduplicates by findingId; cross-model attribution via lineage labels.
   523	 * Returns the merged registry object.
   524	 */
   525	function mergeResearchRegistries(lineageData, options = {}) {
   526	  const mergeOptions = resolveMergeOptions(options);
   527	  const findingById = mergeOptions.enableNearDuplicateDedup ? createFindingBucketIndex() : new Map();
   528	  const schemaWarnings = [];
   529	
   530	  for (const { label, registry: rawRegistry } of lineageData) {
   531	    const { registry, warnings } = normalizeRegistrySchema(rawRegistry, {
   532	      canonicalKey: 'keyFindings',
   533	      aliases: { findings: 'keyFindings' },
   534	      lineage: label,
   535	    });
   536	    for (const w of warnings) {
   537	      schemaWarnings.push(w);
   538	      process.stderr.write(JSON.stringify(w) + '\n');
   539	    }
   540	    if (!registry || !Array.isArray(registry.keyFindings)) continue;
   541	    for (const finding of registry.keyFindings) {
   542	      const id = finding.id || finding.title;
   543	      if (!id) continue;
   544	      if (mergeOptions.enableNearDuplicateDedup) {
   545	        addResearchFinding(getFindingBucket(findingById, id, finding, true).records, finding, label, mergeOptions);
   546	      } else {
   547	        if (!findingById.has(id)) findingById.set(id, []);
   548	        addResearchFinding(findingById.get(id), finding, label, mergeOptions);
   549	      }
   550	    }
   551	  }
   552	
   553	  const mergedFindings = mergeOptions.enableNearDuplicateDedup
   554	    ? flattenFindingBucketIndex(findingById, 'id', ['id', 'title'])
   555	    : flattenFindingBuckets(findingById, 'id', ['id', 'title']);
   556	  const openQuestionsById = new Map();
   557	  const resolvedQuestionsById = new Map();
   558	  const ruledOutById = new Map();
   559	
   560	  for (const { label, registry } of lineageData) {
   561	    if (!registry) continue;
   562	    for (const q of registry.openQuestions ?? []) {
   563	      const id = q.id || q.question || q.text;
   564	      if (!id) continue;
   565	      if (!openQuestionsById.has(id)) openQuestionsById.set(id, { ...q, _lineages: [label] });
   566	      else {
   567	        const existing = openQuestionsById.get(id);
   568	        addLineage(existing, label);
   569	      }
   570	    }
   571	    // Resolved questions are produced per-lineage by the research reducer but
   572	    // were previously dropped here, under-reporting answered coverage in the
   573	    // merged registry. Collect them with the same id/_lineages discipline.
   574	    for (const q of registry.resolvedQuestions ?? []) {
   575	      const id = q.id || q.question || q.text;
   576	      if (!id) continue;
   577	      if (!resolvedQuestionsById.has(id)) resolvedQuestionsById.set(id, { ...q, _lineages: [label] });
   578	      else {
   579	        const existing = resolvedQuestionsById.get(id);
   580	        addLineage(existing, label);
   581	      }
   582	    }
   583	    for (const d of registry.ruledOutDirections ?? []) {
   584	      const id = d.id || d.direction;
   585	      if (!id) continue;
   586	      if (!ruledOutById.has(id)) ruledOutById.set(id, { ...d, _lineages: [label] });
   587	    }
   588	  }
   589	
   590	  const totalIters = lineageData.reduce((sum, { registry }) => {
   591	    return sum + (registry?.metrics?.iterationsCompleted ?? 0);
   592	  }, 0);
   593	
   594	  const avgConvergence =
   595	    lineageData.length > 0
   596	      ? lineageData.reduce((sum, { registry }) => sum + (registry?.metrics?.convergenceScore ?? 0), 0) /
   597	        lineageData.length
   598	      : 0;
   599	
   600	  return {
   601	    mergedFrom: lineageData.map(({ label }) => label).sort(),
   602	    openQuestions: sortByContentThenId([...openQuestionsById.values()], ['id', 'question', 'text']),
   603	    resolvedQuestions: sortByContentThenId([...resolvedQuestionsById.values()], ['id', 'question', 'text']),
   604	    keyFindings: mergedFindings,
   605	    ruledOutDirections: sortByContentThenId([...ruledOutById.values()], ['id', 'direction']),
   606	    metrics: {
   607	      iterationsCompleted: totalIters,
   608	      openQuestions: openQuestionsById.size,
   609	      resolvedQuestions: resolvedQuestionsById.size,
   610	      keyFindings: mergedFindings.length,
   611	      convergenceScore: Math.round(avgConvergence * 1000) / 1000,
   612	      coverageBySources: {},
   613	    },
   614	    ...(schemaWarnings.length > 0 ? { schema_mismatch: schemaWarnings } : {}),
   615	  };
   616	}
   617	
   618	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   619	// 4. REVIEW MERGE  (strongest-restriction)
   620	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   621	
   622	/**
   623	 * Merge review findings registries with strongest-restriction severity rollup.
   624	 * Any lineage with an active P0 finding causes the merged result to be FAIL.
   625	 * Deduplication is by findingId; cross-lineage P0 wins if any lineage reports it.
   626	 */
   627	function mergeReviewRegistries(lineageData, options = {}) {
   628	  const mergeOptions = resolveMergeOptions(options);
   629	  const findingById = mergeOptions.enableNearDuplicateDedup ? createFindingBucketIndex() : new Map();
   630	  const schemaWarnings = [];
   631	
   632	  for (const { label, registry: rawRegistry } of lineageData) {
   633	    const { registry, warnings } = normalizeRegistrySchema(rawRegistry, {
   634	      canonicalKey: 'openFindings',
   635	      aliases: { findings: 'openFindings' },
   636	      lineage: label,
   637	    });
   638	    for (const w of warnings) {
   639	      schemaWarnings.push(w);
   640	      process.stderr.write(JSON.stringify(w) + '\n');
   641	    }
   642	    if (!registry || !Array.isArray(registry.openFindings)) continue;
   643	    for (const finding of registry.openFindings) {
   644	      if (finding.status !== 'active') continue;
   645	      const id = finding.findingId || finding.title;
   646	      if (!id) continue;
   647	      if (mergeOptions.enableNearDuplicateDedup) {
   648	        addReviewFinding(getFindingBucket(findingById, id, finding, true).records, finding, label, mergeOptions);
   649	      } else {
   650	        if (!findingById.has(id)) findingById.set(id, []);
   651	        addReviewFinding(findingById.get(id), finding, label, mergeOptions);
   652	      }
   653	    }
   654	  }
   655	
   656	  // Resolved findings are tracked separately per lineage and were previously
   657	  // dropped here, zeroing the merged resolved coverage. Collect them by id with
   658	  // _lineages attribution, without touching open-finding/verdict semantics.
   659	  const resolvedFindingById = mergeOptions.enableNearDuplicateDedup ? createFindingBucketIndex() : new Map();
   660	  for (const { label, registry } of lineageData) {
   661	    if (!registry || !Array.isArray(registry.resolvedFindings)) continue;
   662	    for (const finding of registry.resolvedFindings) {
   663	      const id = finding.findingId || finding.title;
   664	      if (!id) continue;
   665	      if (mergeOptions.enableNearDuplicateDedup) {
   666	        addReviewFinding(getFindingBucket(resolvedFindingById, id, finding, true).records, finding, label, mergeOptions);
   667	      } else if (resolvedFindingById.has(id)) {
   668	        addLineage(resolvedFindingById.get(id), label);
   669	      } else {
   670	        resolvedFindingById.set(id, { ...finding, _lineages: [label] });
   671	      }
   672	    }
   673	  }
   674	  const mergedResolvedFindings = mergeOptions.enableNearDuplicateDedup
   675	    ? flattenFindingBucketIndex(resolvedFindingById, 'findingId', ['findingId', 'title'])
   676	    : sortByContentThenId([...resolvedFindingById.values()], ['findingId', 'title']);
   677	
   678	  const mergedFindings = mergeOptions.enableNearDuplicateDedup
   679	    ? flattenFindingBucketIndex(findingById, 'findingId', ['findingId', 'title'])
   680	    : flattenFindingBuckets(findingById, 'findingId', ['findingId', 'title']);
   681	  const activeP0 = mergedFindings.filter((f) => f.severity === 'P0' && f.status === 'active').length;
   682	  const activeP1 = mergedFindings.filter((f) => f.severity === 'P1' && f.status === 'active').length;
   683	  const activeP2 = mergedFindings.filter((f) => f.severity === 'P2' && f.status === 'active').length;
   684	
   685	  // Strongest-restriction verdict
   686	  let mergedVerdict;
   687	  if (activeP0 > 0) {
   688	    mergedVerdict = 'FAIL';
   689	  } else if (activeP1 > 0) {
   690	    mergedVerdict = 'CONDITIONAL';
   691	  } else {
   692	    mergedVerdict = 'PASS';
   693	  }
   694	
   695	  return {
   696	    mergedFrom: lineageData.map(({ label }) => label).sort(),
   697	    mergedVerdict,
   698	    openFindings: mergedFindings,
   699	    resolvedFindings: mergedResolvedFindings,
   700	    findingsBySeverity: { P0: activeP0, P1: activeP1, P2: activeP2 },
   701	    openFindingsCount: mergedFindings.length,
   702	    resolvedFindingsCount: mergedResolvedFindings.length,
   703	    activeP0,
   704	    activeP1,
   705	    activeP2,
   706	    ...(schemaWarnings.length > 0 ? { schema_mismatch: schemaWarnings } : {}),
   707	  };
   708	}
   709	
   710	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   906	async function main() {
   907	  const { writeStateAtomic, writeTextAtomic } = await import('../lib/deep-loop/atomic-state.ts');
   908	  const args = parseArgs();
   909	  const loopType = ensureString(args, 'loopType');
   910	  if (loopType !== 'research' && loopType !== 'review' && loopType !== 'context') {
   911	    throw inputError('loopType must be "research", "review", or "context"');
   912	  }
   913	  const artifactDir = ensureString(args, 'artifactDir');
   914	  const lineagesDir = path.join(artifactDir, 'lineages');
   915	
   916	  if (!fs.existsSync(lineagesDir)) {
   917	    jsonOut({ status: 'ok', message: 'no lineages directory — nothing to merge', merged: 0 });
   918	    return;
   919	  }
   920	
   921	  const labelDirs = fs.readdirSync(lineagesDir)
   922	    .filter((entry) => fs.statSync(path.join(lineagesDir, entry)).isDirectory())
   923	    .sort();
   924	
   925	  if (labelDirs.length === 0) {
   926	    jsonOut({ status: 'ok', message: 'no lineage subdirs found', merged: 0 });
   927	    return;
   928	  }
   929	
   930	  // Load per-lineage data
   931	  const registryName =
   932	    loopType === 'review' ? 'deep-review-findings-registry.json' : 'deep-research-findings-registry.json';
   933	  const stateLogName = loopType === 'review' ? 'deep-review-state.jsonl' : 'deep-research-state.jsonl';
   934	  const summaryPath = path.join(artifactDir, 'orchestration-summary.json');
   935	  const orchestrationSummary = tryReadJson(summaryPath) ?? {};
   936	
   937	  const lineageData = labelDirs.map((label) => {
   938	    const lineageDir = path.join(lineagesDir, label);
   939	    let registry = tryReadJson(path.join(lineageDir, registryName));
   940	    const stateRecords = readStateLog(path.join(lineageDir, stateLogName));
   941	    // Leaf-only review/research lineages (orchestrator-managed direct-leaf convention) may carry
   942	    // active findings only in their state log's findingDetails, with no registry file.
   943	    // Without a registry, such a lineage was silently skipped by the registry-only merge,
   944	    // dropping its findings from synthesis. Reconstruct a minimal registry from the
   945	    // state log so leaf-only lineages reach merge without a separate reducer step.
   946	    if (!registry && loopType === 'review') {
   947	      registry = reconstructReviewRegistryFromState(stateRecords, label);
   948	    }
   949	    if (!registry && loopType === 'research') {
   950	      registry = reconstructResearchRegistryFromState(stateRecords, label);
   951	    }
   952	    // Infer kind/model from state log executor records
   953	    const executorRecord = stateRecords.find((r) => r.type === 'event' && r.event === 'executor_start');
   954	    return {
   955	      label,
   956	      lineageDir,
   957	      registry,
   958	      stateRecords,
   959	      kind: executorRecord?.kind ?? orchestrationSummary?.[label]?.kind ?? 'unknown',
   960	      model: executorRecord?.model ?? orchestrationSummary?.[label]?.model ?? 'unknown',
   961	    };
   962	  });
   963	
   964	  const lineagesWithRegistry = lineageData.filter((d) => d.registry !== null);
   965	
   966	  let mergedRegistry;
   967	  if (loopType === 'review') {
   968	    mergedRegistry = mergeReviewRegistries(lineagesWithRegistry, resolveMergeOptions(args));
   969	  } else {
   970	    mergedRegistry = mergeResearchRegistries(lineagesWithRegistry, resolveMergeOptions(args));
   971	  }
   972	
   973	  // Write merged registry to base artifact dir (replacing single-executor path).
   974	  // Atomic temp+fsync+rename so a mid-write kill never hands synthesis a
   975	  // truncated registry — readers see the prior file or the complete new one.
   976	  const mergedRegistryPath = path.join(artifactDir, registryName);
   977	  writeStateAtomic(mergedRegistryPath, mergedRegistry);
   978	
   979	  // Write attribution markdown atomically (same torn-write guarantee; text, not JSON).
   980	  const attributionPath = path.join(artifactDir, 'fanout-attribution.md');
   981	  writeTextAtomic(attributionPath, buildAttributionMd(lineageData, loopType));
   982	
   983	  jsonOut({
   984	    status: 'ok',
   985	    loop_type: loopType,
   986	    merged_lineages: lineagesWithRegistry.length,
   987	    skipped_no_registry: lineageData.length - lineagesWithRegistry.length,
   988	    merged_registry_path: mergedRegistryPath,
   989	    attribution_path: attributionPath,
   990	    ...(loopType === 'review'
   991	      ? { merged_verdict: mergedRegistry.mergedVerdict, active_p0: mergedRegistry.activeP0, active_p1: mergedRegistry.activeP1 }
   992	      : { key_findings: mergedRegistry.keyFindings?.length ?? 0 }),
   993	  });
   994	}

    54	function normalizeConcurrency(value) {
    55	  const n = Number(value);
    56	  if (!Number.isFinite(n) || n < 1) {
    57	    return 1;
    58	  }
    59	  return Math.floor(n);
    60	}
    61	
    62	function labelFor(item, index) {
    63	  if (isRecord(item) && typeof item.label === 'string' && item.label.trim() !== '') {
    64	    return item.label;
    65	  }
    66	  return `item-${index}`;
    67	}
    68	
    69	function buildPoolGauges({ total, settled, pending, failed, oldestPendingLagMs }) {
    70	  const gauges = {
    71	    lag: Math.max(0, total - settled),
    72	    pending: Math.max(0, pending),
    73	    failed: Math.max(0, failed),
    74	  };
    75	  if (Number.isFinite(oldestPendingLagMs)) {
    76	    gauges.oldest_pending_lag_ms = Math.max(0, Math.floor(oldestPendingLagMs));
    77	  }
    78	  return gauges;
    79	}
    80	
    81	function normalizeMaxRetries(value) {
    82	  if (value === undefined || value === null) {
    83	    return 0;
    84	  }
    85	  const n = Number(value);
    86	  if (!Number.isFinite(n) || n < 0) {
    87	    return 0;
    88	  }
    89	  return Math.floor(n);
    90	}
    91	
    92	function normalizeNonNegativeDuration(value) {
    93	  if (value === undefined || value === null) {
    94	    return 0;
    95	  }
    96	  const n = Number(value);
    97	  if (!Number.isFinite(n) || n < 0) {
    98	    return 0;
    99	  }
   100	  return Math.floor(n);
   101	}
   102	
   103	function normalizeLagCeilingAction(value) {
   104	  if (value === undefined || value === null || value === '') {
   105	    return null;
   106	  }
   107	  const action = String(value).trim();
   108	  if (action !== LAG_CEILING_ACTION_ABORT_REQUEUE) {
   109	    throw new TypeError('lagCeilingAction must be "abort-requeue" when set');
   110	  }
   111	  return action;
   112	}
   113	
   114	function normalizeRetryCountMap(value) {
   115	  const counts = new Map();
   116	  if (!isRecord(value)) {
   117	    return counts;
   118	  }
   119	  for (const [label, count] of Object.entries(value)) {
   120	    const n = Number(count);
   121	    if (typeof label === 'string' && label && Number.isFinite(n) && n > 0) {
   122	      counts.set(label, Math.floor(n));
   123	    }
   124	  }
   125	  return counts;
   126	}
   127	
   128	function buildFailureClassRollup(results) {
   129	  const rollup = {
   130	    timeout: 0,
   131	    exit: 0,
   132	    salvage_miss: 0,
   133	  };
   134	  for (const result of results) {
   135	    if (!result || result.status !== 'rejected') {
   136	      continue;
   137	    }
   138	    const failureClass = result.error && typeof result.error.failure_class === 'string'
   139	      ? result.error.failure_class
   140	      : 'exit';
   141	    if (Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(rollup, failureClass)) {
   142	      rollup[failureClass] += 1;
   143	    }
   144	  }
   145	  return rollup;
   146	}
   147	
   148	function normalizeError(error) {
   149	  const classification = classifyLineageFailure(error);
   150	  const reason = error && typeof error === 'object' && typeof error.reason === 'string'
   326	 * failing lineage cannot sink the whole pool (mirrors council `settleSeat`).
   327	 *
   328	 * @param {Object} params - Settlement parameters.
   329	 * @param {*} params.item - The work item (e.g. a lineage descriptor).
   330	 * @param {number} params.index - Zero-based item index (preserves order).
   331	 * @param {Function} params.worker - Async worker: (item, { index, signal? }) => output.
   332	 * @param {Function} params.now - Callable returning a Date or ISO timestamp.
   333	 * @param {Function} [params.onEvent] - Optional callback for ledger events.
   334	 * @param {AbortSignal} [params.abortSignal] - Optional per-attempt abort signal.
   335	 * @param {number} [params.attempt] - One-based attempt number.
   336	 * @param {boolean} [params.emitSettledEvent] - False when the caller emits terminal events.
   337	 * @param {string} [params.startedAtIso] - Stable started timestamp for synthetic settlement.
   338	 * @param {number} [params.startedAtMs] - Stable started clock for duration accounting.
   339	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} Result with label, status, timing, output|error.
   340	 */
   341	async function settleItem({
   342	  item,
   343	  index,
   344	  worker,
   345	  now,
   346	  onEvent,
   347	  attempt: rawAttempt,
   348	  emitSettledEvent: shouldEmitSettledEvent,
   349	  abortSignal,
   350	  startedAtIso: rawStartedAtIso,
   351	  startedAtMs: rawStartedAtMs,
   352	}) {
   353	  const attempt = Number.isFinite(Number(rawAttempt)) ? Number(rawAttempt) : 1;
   354	  const emitSettledEvent = shouldEmitSettledEvent !== false;
   355	  const label = labelFor(item, index);
   356	  const startedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(rawStartedAtIso ?? now());
   357	  const startedAtMs = Number.isFinite(Number(rawStartedAtMs)) ? Number(rawStartedAtMs) : Date.now();
   358	
   359	  if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
   360	    onEvent({ event: 'started', label, index, attempt, at: startedAtIso });
   361	  }
   362	
   363	  try {
   364	    const baseContext = { index, label, attempt };
   365	    const workerContext = abortSignal ? { ...baseContext, signal: abortSignal } : baseContext;
   366	    const output = await worker(item, workerContext);
   367	    const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
   368	    const durationMs = Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs);
   369	    if (emitSettledEvent && typeof onEvent === 'function') {
   370	      onEvent({ event: 'completed', label, index, attempt, at: completedAtIso, duration_ms: durationMs });
   371	    }
   372	    return {
   373	      label,
   374	      status: 'fulfilled',
   375	      attempt,
   376	      started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
   377	      completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
   378	      duration_ms: durationMs,
   379	      output,
   380	    };
   381	  } catch (error) {
   382	    const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
   383	    const durationMs = Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs);
   384	    const normalizedError = normalizeError(error);
   385	    if (emitSettledEvent && typeof onEvent === 'function') {
   386	      onEvent({ event: 'failed', label, index, attempt, at: completedAtIso, duration_ms: durationMs, error: normalizedError });
   387	    }
   388	    return {
   389	      label,
   390	      status: 'rejected',
   391	      attempt,
   392	      started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
   393	      completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
   394	      duration_ms: durationMs,
   395	      error: normalizedError,
   396	    };
   397	  }
   398	}
   399	
   400	/**
   401	 * Run items through a worker with at most `concurrency` in flight.
   402	 *
   403	 * This is the fan-out generalization of council `dispatchCouncilSeats`: same
   404	 * never-throws per-item settlement and ordered results, plus a concurrency cap
   405	 * so a large lineage set runs K-at-a-time (the proven `xargs -P K` shape).
   406	 *
   407	 * @param {Object} options - Pool configuration.
   408	 * @param {Array} options.items - Work items (each ideally has a `label`).
   409	 * @param {number} options.concurrency - Max items in flight (clamped to >= 1).
   410	 * @param {Function} options.worker - Async worker: (item, { index, signal? }) => output.
   411	 * @param {Function} [options.now] - Callable returning a Date/ISO timestamp.
   412	 * @param {Function} [options.onEvent] - Optional ledger-event callback.
   413	 * @param {string} [options.lagCeilingAction] - Optional `abort-requeue` action.
   414	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} { results: [...ordered], summary: { total,
   415	 *   succeeded, failed, all_failed } }.
   416	 * @throws {TypeError} If items is not an array or worker is not a function.
   417	 */
   418	function runCappedPool(options) {
   419	  if (!isRecord(options)) {
   420	    throw new TypeError('runCappedPool options must be an object');
   421	  }
   422	  const { items, worker } = options;
   423	  if (!Array.isArray(items)) {
   424	    throw new TypeError('items must be an array');
   425	  }
   426	  if (typeof worker !== 'function') {
   427	    throw new TypeError('worker must be a function');
   428	  }
   429	
   430	  const concurrency = normalizeConcurrency(options.concurrency);
   431	  const now = typeof options.now === 'function' ? options.now : () => new Date();
   432	  const onEvent = typeof options.onEvent === 'function' ? options.onEvent : undefined;
   433	  const maxRetries = normalizeMaxRetries(options.maxRetries);
   434	  const retryCounts = normalizeRetryCountMap(options.initialRetryCounts);
   435	  const results = new Array(items.length);
   436	  const queue = items.map((_item, index) => index);
   437	  const lagCeilingMs = normalizeNonNegativeDuration(options.lagCeilingMs ?? options.lag_ceiling_ms ?? options.lag_ceiling);
   438	  const lagCeilingAction = normalizeLagCeilingAction(options.lagCeilingAction ?? options.lag_ceiling_action);
   439	  const shouldAbortStalledLineages = lagCeilingAction === LAG_CEILING_ACTION_ABORT_REQUEUE;
   440	  const postExitGraceMs = normalizeNonNegativeDuration(
   441	    options.postExitGraceMs ?? options.post_exit_grace_ms ?? options.postSubprocessExitGraceMs,
   442	  );
   443	  const postExitPollMs = normalizePositiveDuration(
   444	    options.postExitPollMs ?? options.post_exit_poll_ms,
   445	    postExitGraceMs > 0 ? Math.min(postExitGraceMs, 1000) : 1000,
   446	  );
   447	  const getAttemptLiveness = typeof options.getAttemptLiveness === 'function'
   448	    ? options.getAttemptLiveness
   449	    : null;
   450	  const shouldWatchPostExitOrphans = postExitGraceMs > 0 && getAttemptLiveness !== null;
   451	  if (shouldAbortStalledLineages && lagCeilingMs <= 0) {
   452	    throw new TypeError('lagCeilingAction "abort-requeue" requires lagCeilingMs > 0');
   453	  }
   454	  let lagCeilingTimer = null;
   455	  let postExitWatchdogTimer = null;
   456	  let lagCeilingExceeded = false;
   457	  let nextAttemptId = 1;
   458	
   459	  // STALL DETECTOR (not queue backpressure). The lag metric is the time since the pool
   460	  // last made progress — i.e. since the last item settled (or pool start if nothing has
   461	  // settled yet) — measured ONLY while work is still pending. On a healthy fan-out wider
   462	  // than its concurrency, items settle regularly, so each completion resets this clock and
   463	  // the gap stays small: normal second-wave backpressure does NOT trip the ceiling. The
   464	  // gap only grows past the ceiling when NOTHING settles while work waits — a genuinely
   465	  // stalled tail (a worker hung holding a slot). This deliberately replaces the prior
   466	  // time-since-queued-at-pool-start metric, which conflated normal backpressure with a
   467	  // stall and false-fired on every width>concurrency pool.
   468	  let lastProgressAtMs = Date.now();
   469	  const markProgress = () => {
   470	    lastProgressAtMs = Date.now();
   471	  };
   472	
   473	  const oldestPendingLagMs = () => {
   474	    // A stall is only meaningful while there is still queued work waiting for a slot.
   475	    if (lagCeilingMs <= 0 || queue.length === 0) return undefined;
   476	    return Date.now() - lastProgressAtMs;
   477	  };
   478	
   479	  const buildCurrentGauges = () => {
   480	    const settled = results.filter(Boolean).length;
   481	    const failed = results.filter((result) => result && result.status === 'rejected').length;
   482	    return buildPoolGauges({
   483	      total: items.length,
   484	      settled,
   485	      pending: queue.length,
   486	      failed,
   487	      oldestPendingLagMs: oldestPendingLagMs(),
   488	    });
   489	  };
   490	
   491	  return new Promise((resolve) => {
   492	    if (items.length === 0) {
   493	      resolve(buildPoolSummary(results));
   494	      return;
   495	    }
   496	
   497	    let active = 0;
   498	    let resolved = false;
   499	    const activeAttempts = new Map();
   500	    const clearLagCeilingTimer = () => {
   501	      if (lagCeilingTimer) {
   502	        clearTimeout(lagCeilingTimer);
   503	        lagCeilingTimer = null;
   504	      }
   505	    };
   506	    const clearPostExitWatchdogTimer = () => {
   507	      if (postExitWatchdogTimer) {
   508	        clearTimeout(postExitWatchdogTimer);
   509	        postExitWatchdogTimer = null;
   510	      }
   511	    };
   512	
   513	    const emitLagCeilingWarning = (gauges = buildCurrentGauges()) => {
   514	      if (!onEvent || lagCeilingMs <= 0 || lagCeilingExceeded || queue.length === 0) return;
   515	      if ((gauges.oldest_pending_lag_ms ?? 0) < lagCeilingMs) return;
   516	      lagCeilingExceeded = true;
   517	      onEvent({
   518	        event: 'lag_ceiling_exceeded',
   519	        at: normalizeTimestamp(now()),
   520	        severity: 'warning',
   521	        ...(lagCeilingAction ? { action: lagCeilingAction } : {}),
   522	        // The lag metric is time-since-last-progress while work is pending: a genuine
   523	        // stall signal, not queue backpressure. oldest_pending_lag_ms keeps its name for
   524	        // ledger shape compatibility but now carries the stall duration.
   525	        metric: 'time_since_last_completion',
   526	        lag_ceiling_ms: lagCeilingMs,
   527	        oldest_pending_lag_ms: gauges.oldest_pending_lag_ms,
   528	        gauges,
   529	      });
   530	    };
   531	
   532	    const scheduleLagCeilingCheck = () => {
   533	      clearLagCeilingTimer();
   534	      if (lagCeilingMs <= 0 || queue.length === 0) return;
   535	      if (!onEvent && !shouldAbortStalledLineages) return;
   536	      if (!shouldAbortStalledLineages && lagCeilingExceeded) return;
   537	      const lag = oldestPendingLagMs() ?? 0;
   538	      const delayMs = Math.max(0, lagCeilingMs - lag);
   539	      lagCeilingTimer = setTimeout(() => {
   540	        lagCeilingTimer = null;
   541	        handleLagCeilingExceeded();
   542	        if (!resolved) {
   543	          scheduleLagCeilingCheck();
   544	        }
   545	      }, delayMs);
   546	      lagCeilingTimer.unref?.();
   547	    };
   548	
   549	    const emitEvent = onEvent
   550	      ? (event) => {
   551	          onEvent({
   552	            ...event,
   553	            gauges: buildCurrentGauges(),
   554	          });
   555	          emitLagCeilingWarning();
   556	        }
   557	      : undefined;
   558	
   559	    const buildSyntheticAbortResult = (activeAttempt, gauges, error) => {
   560	      const lagMs = gauges.oldest_pending_lag_ms ?? lagCeilingMs;
   561	      const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
   562	      const durationMs = Math.max(0, Date.now() - activeAttempt.startedAtMs);
   563	      return {
   564	        label: activeAttempt.label,
   565	        status: 'rejected',
   566	        index: activeAttempt.index,
   567	        attempt: activeAttempt.attempt,
   568	        started_at_iso: activeAttempt.startedAtIso,
   569	        completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
   570	        duration_ms: durationMs,
   571	        error: {
   572	          ...normalizeError(error),
   573	          aborted: true,
   574	          abort_reason: 'lag_ceiling_exceeded',
   575	        },
   576	      };
   577	    };
   578	
   579	    const buildPostExitOrphanResult = (activeAttempt, liveness, error) => {
   580	      const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
   581	      const durationMs = Math.max(0, Date.now() - activeAttempt.startedAtMs);
   582	      return {
   583	        label: activeAttempt.label,
   584	        status: 'rejected',
   585	        index: activeAttempt.index,
   586	        attempt: activeAttempt.attempt,
   587	        started_at_iso: activeAttempt.startedAtIso,
   588	        completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
   589	        duration_ms: durationMs,
   590	        error: {
   591	          ...normalizeError(error),
   592	          reason: POST_EXIT_ORPHAN_REASON,
   593	          post_exit_grace_ms: postExitGraceMs,
   594	          ...(Number.isInteger(liveness.pid) && liveness.pid > 0 ? { pid: liveness.pid } : {}),
   595	          ...(Number.isFinite(liveness.exitedAtMs) ? { subprocess_exited_at_ms: Math.floor(liveness.exitedAtMs) } : {}),
   596	        },
   597	      };
   598	    };
   599	
   600	    const emitSettledResult = (result, isTerminal) => {
   601	      if (!emitEvent) return;
   602	      const baseEvent = {
   603	        event: result.status === 'fulfilled' ? 'completed' : 'failed',
   604	        label: result.label,
   605	        index: result.index,
   606	        attempt: result.attempt,
   607	        at: result.completed_at_iso,
   608	        duration_ms: result.duration_ms,
   609	        terminal: isTerminal,
   610	      };
   611	      if (result.status === 'rejected') {
   612	        emitEvent({ ...baseEvent, error: result.error });
   613	      } else {
   614	        emitEvent(baseEvent);
   615	      }
   616	    };
   617	
   618	    const handleSettledResult = (activeAttempt, result) => {
   619	      const { index, label, retryCount } = activeAttempt;
   620	      result.index = index;
   621	      if (result.status === 'fulfilled') {
   622	        result.retry_attempts = retryCount;
   623	        results[index] = result;
   624	        emitSettledResult(result, true);
   625	        return;
   626	      }
   627	
   628	      const canRetry = result.error.retryable === true && retryCount < maxRetries;
   629	      if (canRetry) {
   630	        const nextRetryCount = retryCount + 1;
   631	        retryCounts.set(label, nextRetryCount);
   632	        emitSettledResult(result, false);
   633	        queue.push(index);
   634	        if (emitEvent) {
   635	          emitEvent({
   636	            event: 'retry_scheduled',
   637	            label,
   638	            index,
   639	            at: normalizeTimestamp(now()),
   640	            retry_count: nextRetryCount,
   641	            next_attempt: nextRetryCount + 1,
   642	            max_retries: maxRetries,
   643	            failure_class: result.error.failure_class,
   644	            retry_verdict: result.error.retry_verdict,
   645	          });
   646	        }
   647	        return;
   648	      }
   649	
   650	      result.retry_attempts = retryCount;
   651	      result.retry_exhausted = result.error.retryable === true && retryCount >= maxRetries;
   652	      results[index] = result;
   653	      emitSettledResult(result, true);
   654	    };
   655	
   656	    const buildPoolErrorResult = (activeAttempt, error) => ({
   657	      label: activeAttempt.label,
   658	      status: 'rejected',
   659	      index: activeAttempt.index,
   660	      attempt: activeAttempt.attempt,
   661	      started_at_iso: activeAttempt.startedAtIso,
   662	      completed_at_iso: normalizeTimestamp(now()),
   663	      duration_ms: Math.max(0, Date.now() - activeAttempt.startedAtMs),
   664	      error: normalizeError({ name: 'PoolError', message: String(error) }),
   665	    });
   666	
   667	    const settleActiveAttempt = (activeAttempt, result) => {
   668	      if (activeAttempt.settled) {
   669	        return;
   670	      }
   671	      activeAttempt.settled = true;
   672	      activeAttempts.delete(activeAttempt.id);
   673	      handleSettledResult(activeAttempt, result);
   674	      active -= 1;
   675	      // Every settlement (success, failure, or retry re-queue) is forward progress:
   676	      // reset the stall clock so a healthy pool's steady completions keep the lag
   677	      // metric small and only a true stall (no settlement) lets it grow.
   678	      markProgress();
   679	      scheduleLagCeilingCheck();
   680	      pump();
   681	    };
   682	
   683	    const abortStalledAttempt = (gauges) => {
   684	      if (!shouldAbortStalledLineages || activeAttempts.size === 0 || queue.length === 0) return false;
   685	      if ((gauges.oldest_pending_lag_ms ?? 0) < lagCeilingMs) return false;
   686	      const activeAttempt = activeAttempts.values().next().value;
   687	      if (!activeAttempt || activeAttempt.settled) return false;
   688	
   689	      const error = buildLagCeilingTimeoutError({
   690	        label: activeAttempt.label,
   691	        lagMs: gauges.oldest_pending_lag_ms ?? lagCeilingMs,
   692	        lagCeilingMs,
   693	      });
   694	      const result = buildSyntheticAbortResult(activeAttempt, gauges, error);
   695	      activeAttempt.abortController.abort(error);
   696	      if (onEvent) {
   697	        onEvent({
   698	          event: 'lag_ceiling_abort',
   699	          label: activeAttempt.label,
   700	          index: activeAttempt.index,
   701	          attempt: activeAttempt.attempt,
   702	          at: result.completed_at_iso,
   703	          action: lagCeilingAction,
   704	          lag_ceiling_ms: lagCeilingMs,
   705	          oldest_pending_lag_ms: gauges.oldest_pending_lag_ms,
   706	          gauges: buildCurrentGauges(),
   707	        });
   708	      }
   709	      settleActiveAttempt(activeAttempt, result);
   710	      return true;
   711	    };
   712	
   713	    const handleLagCeilingExceeded = () => {
   714	      if (lagCeilingMs <= 0 || queue.length === 0) return;
   715	      const gauges = buildCurrentGauges();
   850	      gauges,
   851	      failure_classes: failureClasses,
   852	    },
   853	  };
   854	}
   855	
   856	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   857	// 5. STATUS LEDGER
   858	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   859	
   860	/**
   861	 * Append one JSONL event to the orchestration status ledger.
   862	 *
   863	 * Follows the `orchestration-status.log` ledger pattern: one line per lineage
   864	 * lifecycle event (started / completed / failed / salvaged / converged).
   865	 *
   866	 * @param {string} ledgerPath - Path to the JSONL ledger file.
   867	 * @param {Object} entry - A serializable event object.
   868	 */
   869	function appendStatusLedger(ledgerPath, entry) {
   870	  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(ledgerPath), { recursive: true });
   871	  fs.appendFileSync(ledgerPath, `${JSON.stringify(entry)}\n`);
   872	}
   873	
   874	/**
   875	 * Write the orchestration summary JSON for a completed fan-out run.
   876	 *
   877	 * @param {string} summaryPath - Path to the summary JSON file.
   878	 * @param {Object} summary - Summary payload (e.g. the pool summary plus
   879	 *   salvage counts and per-lineage rollups).
   880	 */
   881	function writeOrchestrationSummary(summaryPath, summary) {
   882	  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(summaryPath), { recursive: true });
   883	  fs.writeFileSync(summaryPath, `${JSON.stringify(summary, null, 2)}\n`);
   884	}
   885	
   886	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   887	// 6. EXPORTS
   888	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   889	
   890	module.exports = {
   891	  runCappedPool,
   892	  settleItem,
   893	  buildPoolSummary,
   894	  buildPoolGauges,
   895	  classifyLineageFailure,
   896	  detectOrphanedLineages,
   897	  createWavePlannerInterface,
   898	  markOrphanedLineages,
   899	  readRetryCountsFromLedger,
   900	  appendStatusLedger,
   901	  writeOrchestrationSummary,
   902	};

    94	
    95	function parseArgs(argv = process.argv.slice(2)) {
    96	  const args = {};
    97	  for (let i = 0; i < argv.length; i += 1) {
    98	    const token = argv[i];
    99	    if (!token.startsWith('--')) {
   100	      throw inputError(`Unexpected positional argument: ${token}`);
   101	    }
   102	    const key = token.slice(2).replace(/-([a-z])/g, (_, c) => c.toUpperCase());
   103	    const next = argv[i + 1];
   104	    if (next === undefined || next.startsWith('--')) {
   105	      args[key] = true;
   106	    } else {
   107	      args[key] = next;
   108	      i += 1;
   109	    }
   110	  }
   111	  return args;
   112	}
   113	
   114	function inputError(message) {
   115	  const err = new Error(message);
   116	  err.code = 'INPUT_VALIDATION';
   117	  return err;
   118	}
   119	
   120	function ensureString(args, key) {
   121	  if (!args[key] || typeof args[key] !== 'string') {
   122	    throw inputError(`${key} is required`);
   123	  }
   124	  return args[key];
   125	}
   126	
   127	function parseOptionalNumber(args, key) {
   128	  const raw = args[key];
   129	  if (raw === undefined || raw === false || raw === null || raw === '') {
   130	    return null;
   131	  }
   132	  if (raw === true || typeof raw !== 'string') {
   133	    throw inputError(`${key} must be a number`);
   134	  }
   135	  const value = Number(raw);
   136	  if (!Number.isFinite(value) || value < 0) {
   137	    throw inputError(`${key} must be a non-negative number`);
   138	  }
   139	  return value;
   140	}
   141	
   142	function readRawConfigNumber(rawConfig, names, fieldName) {
   143	  if (!isRecord(rawConfig)) return null;
   144	  for (const name of names) {
   145	    if (!Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(rawConfig, name)) continue;
   146	    const raw = rawConfig[name];
   147	    if (raw === undefined || raw === null || raw === '') return null;
   148	    if (raw === true || raw === false) {
   149	      throw inputError(`${fieldName} must be a non-negative number`);
   150	    }
   151	    const value = Number(raw);
   152	    if (!Number.isFinite(value) || value < 0) {
   153	      throw inputError(`${fieldName} must be a non-negative number`);
   154	    }
   155	    return Math.floor(value);
   156	  }
   157	  return null;
   158	}
   159	
   160	function readPositiveRawConfigNumber(rawConfig, names, fieldName, fallback) {
   161	  const value = readRawConfigNumber(rawConfig, names, fieldName);
   162	  if (value === null) return fallback;
   163	  if (value <= 0) {
   164	    throw inputError(`${fieldName} must be greater than 0`);
   165	  }
   166	  return value;
   167	}
   168	
   169	function rawConfigWithCliBudgetOverrides(rawConfig, args) {
   170	  const merged = { ...(isRecord(rawConfig) ? rawConfig : {}) };
   171	  const mappings = [
   172	    ['stallWatchdogMs', 'stallWatchdogMs'],
   173	    ['maxCostUnitsPerLineage', 'maxCostUnitsPerLineage'],
   174	    ['maxAggregateCostUnits', 'maxAggregateCostUnits'],
   175	    ['maxTokensPerLineage', 'maxTokensPerLineage'],
   176	    ['maxTokenBudget', 'maxTokenBudget'],
   177	    ['maxTokenBudgetPerLineage', 'maxTokenBudgetPerLineage'],
   178	    ['costUnitsPerIteration', 'costUnitsPerIteration'],
   179	    ['estimatedTokensPerIteration', 'estimatedTokensPerIteration'],
   180	  ];
   181	  for (const [targetKey, argKey] of mappings) {
   182	    if (Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(args, argKey)) {
   183	      merged[targetKey] = args[argKey];
   184	    }
   185	  }
   186	  return merged;
   187	}
   188	
   189	function normalizeStopPolicy(raw) {
   190	  if (raw === undefined || raw === false || raw === null || raw === '') {
   191	    return 'convergence';
   192	  }
   193	  if (raw === true || typeof raw !== 'string') {
   194	    throw inputError('stopPolicy must be convergence or max-iterations');
   195	  }
   196	  if (raw !== 'convergence' && raw !== 'max-iterations') {
   197	    throw inputError('stopPolicy must be convergence or max-iterations');
   198	  }

   200	}
   201	
   202	function jsonOut(payload) {
   203	  process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify(payload)}\n`);

   206	function signalExitCode(signal) {
   207	  return signal === 'SIGINT' ? 130 : 143;
   208	}
   209	
   210	let fanoutSignalHandlersInstalled = false;
   211	
   212	function installFanoutSignalHandlers(onStop) {
   213	  if (fanoutSignalHandlersInstalled) return;
   214	  fanoutSignalHandlersInstalled = true;
   215	
   216	  for (const signal of ['SIGINT', 'SIGTERM']) {
   217	    process.once(signal, () => {
   218	      try {
   219	        onStop(signal);
   220	      } finally {
   221	        process.exit(signalExitCode(signal));
   222	      }
   223	    });
   224	  }
   225	}
   226	
   227	function updateLineageSnapshot(snapshots, event) {
   228	  if (!event || typeof event.label !== 'string') return;
   229	  const existing = snapshots.get(event.label) || { label: event.label };
   230	
   231	  if (event.event === 'started') {
   232	    snapshots.set(event.label, { ...existing, status: 'running', started_at_iso: event.at });
   233	    return;
   234	  }
   235	  if (event.event === 'completed') {
   236	    snapshots.set(event.label, {
   237	      ...existing,
   238	      status: 'fulfilled',
   239	      completed_at_iso: event.at,
   240	      duration_ms: event.duration_ms,
   241	    });
   242	    return;
   243	  }
   244	  if (event.event === 'failed') {
   245	    snapshots.set(event.label, {
   246	      ...existing,
   247	      status: event.terminal === false ? 'retrying' : 'rejected',
   248	      completed_at_iso: event.at,
   249	      duration_ms: event.duration_ms,
   250	      error: event.error,
   251	    });
   252	    return;
   253	  }
   254	  if (event.event === 'retry_scheduled' || event.event === 'orphan_requeued') {
   255	    snapshots.set(event.label, { ...existing, status: 'requeued', updated_at_iso: event.at });
   256	    return;
   257	  }
   258	  if (event.event === 'progress') {
   259	    snapshots.set(event.label, {
   260	      ...existing,
   261	      status: 'running',
   262	      updated_at_iso: event.at,
   263	      duration_ms: event.duration_ms,
   264	    });
   265	  }
   266	}
   267	
   268	function buildLineageSnapshots(lineages, snapshots) {
   269	  return lineages.map((lineage) => snapshots.get(lineage.label) || { label: lineage.label, status: 'pending' });
   270	}
   271	
   272	function summarizeSnapshots(lineages, snapshots) {
   273	  const results = buildLineageSnapshots(lineages, snapshots);
   274	  const succeeded = results.filter((result) => result.status === 'fulfilled').length;
   275	  const failed = results.filter((result) => result.status === 'rejected').length;
   276	  return {
   277	    results,
   278	    summary: {
   279	      total: lineages.length,
   280	      succeeded,
   281	      failed,
   282	      all_failed: lineages.length > 0 && failed === lineages.length,
   283	    },
   284	  };
   285	}
   286	
   287	function observabilityPathForLedger(ledgerPath) {
   288	  return path.join(path.dirname(ledgerPath), OBSERVABILITY_EVENTS_FILENAME);
   289	}
   290	
   291	function statusForLedgerEvent(entry) {
   292	  if (entry && typeof entry.status === 'string' && entry.status.trim() !== '') {
   293	    return entry.status;
   294	  }
   295	  if (!entry || typeof entry.event !== 'string') return 'unknown';
   296	  if (entry.event === 'started' || entry.event === 'progress' || entry.event === 'resume_waiting') return 'running';
   297	  if (entry.event === 'completed' || entry.event === 'resume_waiting_complete') return 'completed';
   298	  if (entry.event === 'failed') return entry.terminal === false ? 'retrying' : 'failed';
   299	  if (entry.event === 'stopped') return 'stopped';
   300	  if (entry.event === 'retry_scheduled' || entry.event === 'orphan_requeued') return 'retrying';
   301	  // Lag-ceiling signals from the pool: a stall warning while work remains pending, or a
   302	  // forced abort of the stalled attempt. Map both to typed statuses instead of 'unknown'.
   303	  if (entry.event === 'lag_ceiling_exceeded') return entry.action === 'abort' ? 'failed' : 'warning';
   304	  if (entry.event === 'lag_ceiling_abort') return 'failed';
   305	  if (entry.event === 'stall_detected') return 'warning';
   306	  if (entry.event === 'budget_cap_exceeded') return 'failed';
   307	  if (entry.event === 'aggregate_budget_cap_exceeded') return 'failed';
   308	  return 'unknown';
   309	}
   310	
   500	function validateClaudeConfigDir(configDir) {
   501	  if (typeof configDir !== 'string' || configDir.trim() === '') {
   502	    throw inputError('configDir must be a non-empty string when set');
   503	  }
   504	  validateNamespaceValue(configDir, 'configDir', inputError);
   505	
   506	  const expanded = expandHomeDir(configDir);
   507	  const resolved = path.resolve(process.cwd(), expanded);
   508	  const homeDir = path.resolve(os.homedir());
   509	  const cwd = path.resolve(process.cwd());
   510	  const homeRelative = path.relative(homeDir, resolved);
   511	  const cwdRelative = path.relative(cwd, resolved);
   512	  const homeTop = homeRelative.split(path.sep)[0] || '';
   513	  const cwdTop = cwdRelative.split(path.sep)[0] || '';
   514	  const allowedConfigRoot = (segment) => segment === '.claude' || segment.startsWith('.claude-');
   515	
   516	  if (isPathInside(resolved, homeDir) && allowedConfigRoot(homeTop)) {
   517	    return resolved;
   518	  }
   519	  if (isPathInside(resolved, cwd) && allowedConfigRoot(cwdTop)) {
   520	    return resolved;
   521	  }
   522	  throw inputError('configDir must resolve under ~/.claude*, or a repo-local .claude* directory');
   523	}
   524	
   525	function terminateLineageProcess(child, signal) {
   526	  if (!child || child.killed) return;
   527	  if (process.platform !== 'win32' && child.pid) {
   528	    try {
   529	      process.kill(-child.pid, signal);
   530	      return;
   531	    } catch (error) {
   532	      if (error && error.code !== 'ESRCH') {
   533	        try {
   534	          child.kill(signal);
   535	        } catch {
   536	          // The process may have already exited between the group and direct kill attempts.
   537	        }
   538	      }
   539	      return;
   540	    }
   541	  }
   542	  try {
   543	    child.kill(signal);
   544	  } catch {
   545	    // The process may have already exited.
   546	  }
   547	}

   549	function expectedLineageArtifactPaths(loopType, lineageDir) {
   550	  assertActiveFanoutLoopType(loopType);

   552	  return [path.join(lineageDir, 'research.md')];
   553	}
   554	
   555	function hasNonEmptyFile(filePath) {
   556	  try {
   557	    return fs.statSync(filePath).isFile() && fs.statSync(filePath).size > 0;
   558	  } catch {
   559	    return false;
   560	  }
   561	}
   562	
   563	function findMissingLineageArtifacts(loopType, lineageDir) {
   564	  return expectedLineageArtifactPaths(loopType, lineageDir)
   565	    .filter((artifactPath) => !hasNonEmptyFile(artifactPath));
   566	}
   567	
   568	function parseJsonlRecords(filePath) {
   569	  const text = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8');
   570	  return text
   571	    .split(/\r?\n/)
   572	    .filter((line) => line.trim() !== '')
   573	    .map((line) => JSON.parse(line));
   574	}
   575	
   576	function lineageStateLogName(loopType) {
   577	  return STATE_LOG_BY_LOOP_TYPE[loopType] || null;
   578	}
   579	
   580	function readLineageStateRecords(loopType, lineageDir) {
   581	  const stateLogName = lineageStateLogName(loopType);
   582	  if (!stateLogName) {
   583	    return { statePath: null, records: [], missing: true, parseError: null };
   584	  }
   585	  const statePath = path.join(lineageDir, stateLogName);
   586	  if (!hasNonEmptyFile(statePath)) {
   587	    return { statePath, records: [], missing: true, parseError: null };
   588	  }
   589	  try {
   590	    return { statePath, records: parseJsonlRecords(statePath), missing: false, parseError: null };
   591	  } catch (error) {
   592	    return {
   593	      statePath,
   594	      records: [],
   595	      missing: false,
   596	      parseError: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
   597	    };
   598	  }
   599	}
   600	
   601	// A lineage's synthesis stopReason is written by its model and cannot be held to an exact
   602	// spelling. The iteration-count check above already proves the lineage ran every iteration,
   603	// so this only needs to confirm the reason belongs to the max-iterations family (e.g.
   604	// "maxIterationsReached", "max-iterations (10/10)") and is not a genuinely different outcome
   605	// such as convergence, a manual stop, or an error.
   606	function isMaxIterationsStopReason(stopReason) {
   607	  if (typeof stopReason !== 'string') return false;
   608	  return stopReason.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z]/g, '').startsWith('maxiteration');
   609	}
   610	
   611	function findMaxIterationsPolicyViolation({ loopType, stateRead, lineage, stopPolicy }) {
   612	  if (loopType !== 'review' || stopPolicy !== 'max-iterations') return null;
   613	  if (typeof lineage.iterations !== 'number' || lineage.iterations <= 0) {
   614	    return 'stopPolicy=max-iterations requires a positive lineage iteration cap';
   615	  }
   616	
   617	  if (!stateRead || stateRead.missing) {
   618	    return 'missing deep-review-state.jsonl for max-iterations stop-policy validation';
   619	  }
   620	  if (stateRead.parseError) {
   621	    return `could not parse deep-review-state.jsonl: ${stateRead.parseError}`;
   622	  }
   623	
   624	  const records = stateRead.records;
   625	  const iterationCount = records.filter((record) => record && record.type === 'iteration').length;
   626	  const synthesis = [...records]
   627	    .reverse()
   628	    .find((record) => record && record.type === 'event' && record.event === 'synthesis_complete');
   629	  if (!synthesis) {
   630	    return 'missing synthesis_complete event for max-iterations stop-policy validation';
   631	  }
   632	
   633	  const totalIterations = Number.isFinite(Number(synthesis.totalIterations))
   634	    ? Number(synthesis.totalIterations)
   635	    : iterationCount;
   636	  if (totalIterations !== lineage.iterations) {
   637	    return `expected ${lineage.iterations} iterations, got ${totalIterations}`;
   638	  }
   639	  if (!isMaxIterationsStopReason(synthesis.stopReason)) {
   640	    return `expected stopReason=maxIterationsReached, got ${synthesis.stopReason || 'unknown'}`;
   641	  }
   642	  return null;
   643	}
   644	
   645	function isRecord(value) {
   646	  return value !== null && typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value);
   647	}
   648	
   649	function normalizeStallWatchdogMs(rawConfig) {
   650	  // Defaults ON (mirrors the fan-out lagCeilingMs default): without a watchdog a stalled
   651	  // lineage hangs silently until the hours-scale timeout. Read through the raw alias set
   652	  // because this knob is intentionally outside fanoutConfigSchema. Non-disableable while
   653	  // running autonomously: an explicit 0 no longer opts out, it is rejected.
   654	  const value = readRawConfigNumber(rawConfig, [
   655	    'stallWatchdogMs',
   656	    'stall_watchdog_ms',
   657	    'stallWatchdogMillis',
   658	    'stall_watchdog_millis',
   659	  ], 'stallWatchdogMs');
   660	  if (value === 0) {
   661	    throw inputError('stallWatchdogMs must be greater than 0; stall detection cannot be disabled for autonomous fan-out runs');
   662	  }
   663	  return value ?? 300000;
   664	}
   665	
   666	const DEFAULT_MAX_COST_UNITS_PER_LINEAGE = 72;
   667	
   668	function normalizeLineageBudgetGuards(rawConfig = {}) {
   669	  return {
   670	    max_cost_units_per_lineage: readRawConfigNumber(rawConfig, [
   671	      'maxCostUnitsPerLineage',
   672	      'max_cost_units_per_lineage',
   673	      'maxLineageCostUnits',
   674	      'max_lineage_cost_units',
   675	      'maxTokensPerLineage',
   676	      'max_tokens_per_lineage',
   677	      'maxTokenBudget',
   678	      'max_token_budget',
   679	      'maxTokenBudgetPerLineage',
   680	      'max_token_budget_per_lineage',
   681	    ], 'maxCostUnitsPerLineage') ?? DEFAULT_MAX_COST_UNITS_PER_LINEAGE,
   682	    cost_units_per_iteration: readPositiveRawConfigNumber(rawConfig, [
   683	      'costUnitsPerIteration',
   684	      'cost_units_per_iteration',
   685	      'estimatedTokensPerIteration',
   686	      'estimated_tokens_per_iteration',
   687	    ], 'costUnitsPerIteration', 1),
   688	  };
   689	}
   690	
   691	function computeLineageBudgetUpperBound(lineage, guardsInput = {}, maxRetries = 0) {
   692	  const guards = normalizeLineageBudgetGuards(guardsInput);
   693	  const rawIterations = Number(lineage && lineage.iterations);
   694	  const iterations = Number.isFinite(rawIterations) && rawIterations > 0 ? Math.floor(rawIterations) : 12;
   695	  const normalizedRetries = Number.isFinite(Number(maxRetries)) && Number(maxRetries) >= 0
   696	    ? Math.floor(Number(maxRetries))
   697	    : 0;
   698	  const totalAttempts = normalizedRetries + 1;
   699	  return {
   700	    ...guards,
   701	    iterations,
   702	    total_attempts: totalAttempts,
   703	    estimated_cost_units: iterations * guards.cost_units_per_iteration * totalAttempts,
   704	  };
   705	}
   706	
   707	function evaluateLineageBudgetCap(input = {}) {
   708	  const upperBound = computeLineageBudgetUpperBound(
   709	    input.lineage || {},
   710	    input.guards || input,
   711	    input.maxRetries,
   712	  );
   713	  const exceeded = upperBound.max_cost_units_per_lineage > 0
   714	    && upperBound.estimated_cost_units > upperBound.max_cost_units_per_lineage;
   715	  return {
   716	    continue_allowed: !exceeded,
   717	    stop_reasons: exceeded ? ['max_cost_units_per_lineage'] : [],
   718	    upper_bound: upperBound,
   719	  };
   720	}
   721	
   722	// Aggregate budget across every expanded lineage in a run. This is a SEPARATE ceiling
   723	// from DEFAULT_MAX_COST_UNITS_PER_LINEAGE above: the per-lineage cap bounds any single
   724	// lineage, this bounds the whole fan-out submission, and both are enforced independently
   725	// (a run may pass one and still fail the other).
   726	const DEFAULT_MAX_AGGREGATE_COST_UNITS = 288;
   727	
   728	function normalizeAggregateBudgetGuards(rawConfig = {}) {
   729	  return {
   730	    max_aggregate_cost_units: readRawConfigNumber(rawConfig, [
   731	      'maxAggregateCostUnits',
   732	      'max_aggregate_cost_units',
   733	      'maxTotalCostUnits',
   734	      'max_total_cost_units',
   735	    ], 'maxAggregateCostUnits') ?? DEFAULT_MAX_AGGREGATE_COST_UNITS,
   736	  };
   737	}
   738	
   739	function computeAggregateBudgetUpperBound(lineages, guardsInput = {}, maxRetries = 0) {
   740	  const aggregateGuards = normalizeAggregateBudgetGuards(guardsInput);
   741	  const lineageGuards = normalizeLineageBudgetGuards(guardsInput);
   742	  const perLineage = (lineages || []).map((lineage) => {
   743	    const upperBound = computeLineageBudgetUpperBound(lineage, lineageGuards, maxRetries);
   744	    return { label: lineage && lineage.label, estimated_cost_units: upperBound.estimated_cost_units };
   745	  });
   746	  const estimatedCostUnits = perLineage.reduce((sum, entry) => sum + entry.estimated_cost_units, 0);
   747	  return {
   748	    max_aggregate_cost_units: aggregateGuards.max_aggregate_cost_units,
   749	    estimated_cost_units: estimatedCostUnits,
   750	    lineage_count: perLineage.length,
   751	    per_lineage: perLineage,
   752	  };
   753	}
   754	
   755	function evaluateAggregateBudgetCap(input = {}) {
   756	  const upperBound = computeAggregateBudgetUpperBound(
   757	    input.lineages || [],
   758	    input.guards || input,
   759	    input.maxRetries,
   760	  );
  1490	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1491	// 4. CORE LOGIC
  1492	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1493	
  1494	async function main() {
  1495	  const args = parseArgs();
  1496	  const specFolder = validateNamespaceValue(ensureString(args, 'specFolder'), 'specFolder', inputError);
  1497	  const loopType = ensureString(args, 'loopType');
  1498	  assertActiveFanoutLoopType(loopType);
  1499	  // Optional shared research question, threaded into each lineage prompt so a research
  1500	  // fan-out can bind research_topic. Absent for review, which scopes by folder.
  1501	  const researchTopic = typeof args.researchTopic === 'string' && args.researchTopic.trim()
  1502	    ? args.researchTopic.trim()
  1503	    : null;
  1504	  const fanoutConfigJson = ensureString(args, 'fanoutConfigJson');
  1505	  const baseArtifactDir = validateBaseArtifactDir(ensureString(args, 'baseArtifactDir'), specFolder, loopType);
  1506	  const convergenceThreshold = parseOptionalNumber(args, 'convergenceThreshold');
  1507	  const lineageTimeoutHoursOverride = parseOptionalNumber(args, 'lineageTimeoutHours');
  1508	  assertLineageTimeoutHoursOverrideWithinCeiling(lineageTimeoutHoursOverride);
  1509	  const stopPolicy = normalizeStopPolicy(args.stopPolicy);
  1510	
  1511	  const {
  1512	    parseFanoutConfig,
  1513	    expandLineages,
  1514	    resolveSandboxMode,
  1515	    resolveClaudePermissionMode,
  1516	  } = await import('../lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts');
  1517	  const { buildExecutorDispatchEnv, detectSameKindFromStack, CLI_DISPATCH_STACK_ENV } = await import('../lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.ts');
  1518	  const {
  1519	    DEFAULT_LINEAGE_TIMESTAMP_TOLERANCE_MS,
  1520	    checkLineageTimestampWindow,
  1521	  } = await import('../lib/deep-loop/lineage-timestamp-window.ts');
  1522	
  1523	  maybeThrowTestFault();
  1524	
  1525	  let rawConfig;
  1526	  try {
  1527	    rawConfig = JSON.parse(fanoutConfigJson);
  1528	  } catch {
  1529	    throw inputError('fanoutConfigJson is not valid JSON');
  1530	  }
  1531	
  1532	  const lineagesDir = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'lineages');
  1533	  const ledgerPath = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'orchestration-status.log');
  1534	  const summaryPath = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'orchestration-summary.json');
  1535	  const runId = `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}`;
  1536	  const parsedFanoutConfig = parseFanoutConfig(rawConfig);
  1537	  const rawGuardConfig = rawConfigWithCliBudgetOverrides(rawConfig, args);
  1538	  const stallWatchdogMs = normalizeStallWatchdogMs(rawGuardConfig);
  1539	  const lineageBudgetGuards = normalizeLineageBudgetGuards(rawGuardConfig);
  1540	  const guardedAssignment = applyFlatPoolAssignmentGuard(parsedFanoutConfig);
  1541	  const fanoutConfig = guardedAssignment.config;
  1542	  const allLineages = expandLineages(fanoutConfig);
  1543	
  1544	  // Aggregate budget is a SEPARATE ceiling from the per-lineage cap enforced inside the
  1545	  // worker below — both must pass, neither replaces the other. Checked upfront (before any
  1546	  // lineage spawns) because it is fully computable from static config, so failing fast here
  1547	  // avoids burning real subprocess time on a run that was already over budget at submission.
  1548	  const aggregateBudgetDecision = evaluateAggregateBudgetCap({
  1549	    lineages: allLineages,
  1550	    guards: rawGuardConfig,
  1551	    maxRetries: fanoutConfig.maxRetries,
  1552	  });
  1553	  if (!aggregateBudgetDecision.continue_allowed) {
  1554	    appendFanoutStatusLedger(ledgerPath, {
  1555	      event: 'aggregate_budget_cap_exceeded',
  1556	      at: new Date().toISOString(),
  1557	      run_id: runId,
  1558	      loop_type: loopType,
  1559	      spec_folder: specFolder,
  1560	      severity: 'error',
  1561	      stop_reasons: aggregateBudgetDecision.stop_reasons,
  1562	      upper_bound: aggregateBudgetDecision.upper_bound,
  1563	    });
  1564	    throw inputError(
  1565	      `fan-out aggregate budget exceeded: ${aggregateBudgetDecision.upper_bound.estimated_cost_units} > `
  1566	        + `${aggregateBudgetDecision.upper_bound.max_aggregate_cost_units} cost units across `
  1567	        + `${aggregateBudgetDecision.upper_bound.lineage_count} lineage(s)`,
  1568	    );
  1569	  }
  1570	
  1571	  const progressHeartbeatMs = normalizeProgressHeartbeatMs(fanoutConfig.progressHeartbeatSeconds);
  1572	  const postExitGraceMs = normalizePostExitGraceMs(rawGuardConfig, progressHeartbeatMs);
  1573	  const slotIntervalMs = progressHeartbeatMs;
  1574	
  1575	  logFlatPoolGuardRejections({
  1576	    rejections: guardedAssignment.rejections,
  1577	    ledgerPath,
  1578	    runId,
  1579	    loopType,
  1580	    specFolder,
  1581	  });
  1582	
  1583	  // Fan-out pool owns every lineage kind. The old YAML-native branch dispatched the
  1584	  // LEAF review agent with a full-loop prompt, which is invalid; keep full-loop
  1585	  // ownership here so all lineages share the same retries and artifact validation.
  1586	  const cliLineages = allLineages;
  1587	  const checkpointPath = waitCheckpointPath(baseArtifactDir);
  1588	  const resumeWaiting = await resumeWaitingCheckpoint({
  1589	    checkpointPath,
  1590	    ledgerPath,
  1591	    runId,
  1592	    loopType,
  1593	    specFolder,
  1594	  });
  1595	  if (!resumeWaiting.didWait) {
  1596	    await persistPreDispatchWait({
  1597	      checkpointPath,
  1598	      ledgerPath,
  1599	      runId,
  1600	      loopType,
  1601	      specFolder,
  1602	      waitMs: args.preDispatchWaitMs,
  1603	    });
  1604	  }
  1605	
  1606	  if (cliLineages.length === 0) {
  1607	    const summary = {
  1608	      total: 0,
  1609	      succeeded: 0,
  1610	      failed: 0,
  1611	      all_failed: false,
  1612	      gauges: { lag: 0, pending: 0, failed: 0 },
  1613	      convergence: { status: 'converged', reason: 'empty_tick', no_new_findings: true },
  1614	    };
  1615	    writeOrchestrationSummary(summaryPath, {
  1616	      run_id: runId,
  1617	      loop_type: loopType,
  1618	      spec_folder: specFolder,
  1619	      base_artifact_dir: baseArtifactDir,
  1620	      total_cli_lineages: 0,
  1621	      ...summary,
  1622	    });
  1623	    jsonOut({ status: 'ok', message: 'no CLI lineages to spawn', run_id: runId, results: [], summary });
  1624	    return;
  1625	  }
  1626	
  1627	  fs.mkdirSync(lineagesDir, { recursive: true });
  1628	  const orphanedLineages = markOrphanedLineages(ledgerPath);
  1629	  const initialRetryCounts = readRetryCountsFromLedger(ledgerPath);
  1630	
  1631	  const lineageSnapshots = new Map();
  1632	  const lineageSlotAccounting = new Map();
  1633	  const lineageProcessLiveness = new Map();
  1634	  let latestGauges = { lag: cliLineages.length, pending: cliLineages.length, failed: 0 };
  1635	  let stoppedSummaryWritten = false;
  1636	
  1637	  const writeStoppedSummary = (signal) => {
  1638	    if (stoppedSummaryWritten) return;
  1639	    stoppedSummaryWritten = true;

 succeeded in 0ms:
#!/usr/bin/env node

// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
// ║ Deep-Loop Runtime — Fan-Out CLI Lineage Pool Runner                      ║
// ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
// ║ Input:  CLI args (--spec-folder, --loop-type, --fanout-config-json,      ║
// ║         --base-artifact-dir).                                            ║
// ║ Output: JSON to stdout.                                                  ║
// ║ Exit:   0=all ok, 1=script error, 2=some failed, 3=all failed.          ║
// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

'use strict';

// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// 1. IMPORTS
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

const fs = require('node:fs');
const os = require('node:os');
const path = require('node:path');
const { spawn, spawnSync } = require('node:child_process');

const {
  classifyExitCode,
  maybeThrowTestFault,
  validateNamespaceValue,
} = require('./lib/cli-guards.cjs');

const {
  runCappedPool,
  appendStatusLedger,
  markOrphanedLineages,
  readRetryCountsFromLedger,
  createWavePlannerInterface,
  writeOrchestrationSummary,
} = require('./fanout-pool.cjs');

const {
  runSalvageSweep,
  extractTextFromOpencodeJson,
} = require('./fanout-salvage.cjs');

const {
  appendObservabilityEvent,
} = require('../lib/deep-loop/observability-events.cjs');

// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// 2. TSX BOOTSTRAP
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

const TSX_LOADER = require.resolve('tsx');
const isTsxLoaded = process.env.DEEP_LOOP_TSX_LOADED === '1';

function runTsxBootstrap() {
  const child = spawn(
    process.execPath,
    ['--import', TSX_LOADER, __filename, ...process.argv.slice(2)],
    {
      cwd: process.cwd(),
      env: { ...process.env, DEEP_LOOP_TSX_LOADED: '1' },
      stdio: [process.stdin.isTTY ? 'ignore' : 'pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
    },
  );

  if (!process.stdin.isTTY && child.stdin) {
    child.stdin.on('error', () => {});
    process.stdin.pipe(child.stdin);
  }

  child.stdout?.pipe(process.stdout);
  child.stderr?.pipe(process.stderr);

  for (const signal of ['SIGINT', 'SIGTERM']) {
    process.once(signal, () => {
      child.kill(signal);
    });
  }

  child.on('close', (status, signal) => {
    if (status !== null) {
      process.exit(status);
    }
    process.exit(signal === 'SIGINT' ? 130 : signal === 'SIGTERM' ? 143 : 1);
  });
}

if (require.main === module && !isTsxLoaded) {
  runTsxBootstrap();
}

   830	
   831	/**
   832	 * Build the ordered-results + summary envelope for a completed pool run.
   833	 *
   834	 * @param {Array<Object>} results - Per-item settlement results (ordered).
   835	 * @returns {Object} { results, summary: { total, succeeded, failed, all_failed } }.
   836	 */
   837	function buildPoolSummary(results) {
   838	  const total = results.length;
   839	  const succeeded = results.filter((result) => result && result.status === 'fulfilled').length;
   840	  const failed = total - succeeded;
   841	  const gauges = buildPoolGauges({ total, settled: total, pending: 0, failed });
   842	  const failureClasses = buildFailureClassRollup(results);
   843	  return {
   844	    results,
   845	    summary: {
   846	      total,
   847	      succeeded,
   848	      failed,
   849	      all_failed: total > 0 && failed === total,
   850	      gauges,
   851	      failure_classes: failureClasses,
   852	    },
   853	  };
   854	}
   855	
   856	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   857	// 5. STATUS LEDGER
   858	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   859	
   860	/**
   861	 * Append one JSONL event to the orchestration status ledger.
   862	 *
   863	 * Follows the `orchestration-status.log` ledger pattern: one line per lineage
   864	 * lifecycle event (started / completed / failed / salvaged / converged).
   865	 *
