     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Deep-Loop Executor Config
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	
     5	import { z } from 'zod';
     6	
     7	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     8	// 1. TYPE DEFINITIONS
     9	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    10	
    11	export const EXECUTOR_KINDS = ['native', 'cli-codex', 'cli-claude-code', 'cli-opencode'] as const;
    12	export type ExecutorKind = typeof EXECUTOR_KINDS[number];
    13	
    14	// Ordered low→high. `ultra` is codex gpt-5.6-sol's top reasoning tier, above `max`.
    15	export const REASONING_EFFORTS = ['none', 'minimal', 'low', 'medium', 'high', 'xhigh', 'max', 'ultra'] as const;
    16	export type ReasoningEffort = typeof REASONING_EFFORTS[number];
    17	
    18	export const SERVICE_TIERS = ['priority', 'standard', 'fast'] as const;
    19	export type ServiceTier = typeof SERVICE_TIERS[number];
    20	
    21	/** Fan-out assignment models accepted by the schema. */
    22	export const FANOUT_ASSIGNMENT_MODELS = ['flat_pool', 'wave'] as const;
    23	
    24	/** Fan-out assignment model selected for a lineage or fan-out block. */
    25	export type FanoutAssignmentModel = typeof FANOUT_ASSIGNMENT_MODELS[number];
    26	
    27	const SANDBOX_MODES = ['read-only', 'workspace-write', 'danger-full-access'] as const;
    28	export type SandboxMode = typeof SANDBOX_MODES[number];
    29	export type ClaudePermissionMode = 'plan' | 'acceptEdits' | 'bypassPermissions';
    30	
    31	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    32	// 2. CONSTANTS
    33	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    34	
    35	export const executorConfigSchema = z.object({
    36	  kind: z.enum(EXECUTOR_KINDS).default('native'),
    37	  model: z.string().min(1).nullable().default(null),
    38	  configDir: z.string().trim().min(1).nullable().default(null),
    39	  reasoningEffort: z.enum(REASONING_EFFORTS).nullable().default(null),
    40	  serviceTier: z.enum(SERVICE_TIERS).nullable().default(null),
    41	  sandboxMode: z.enum(SANDBOX_MODES).nullable().default(null),
    42	  // Hard ceiling of 1 hour per iteration: bounds a single executor invocation so a
    43	  // runaway per-iteration call cannot by itself consume the whole autonomous lifetime budget.
    44	  timeoutSeconds: z.number().int().positive().max(3600).default(900),
    45	  // Optional fable-5 governor capsule for this executor's prompts. Universal and
    46	  // kind-agnostic on purpose: intentionally absent from EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT
    47	  // and the unsupported-field scan, so any executor kind may carry it. null = none.
    48	  governor: z.string().min(1).nullable().default(null),
    49	});
    50	
    51	export type ExecutorConfig = z.infer<typeof executorConfigSchema>;
    52	
    53	export const EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT: Record<ExecutorKind, readonly (keyof ExecutorConfig)[]> = {
    54	  native: [],
    55	  'cli-codex': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'serviceTier', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    56	  'cli-claude-code': ['model', 'configDir', 'reasoningEffort', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    57	  'cli-opencode': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    58	};
    59	
    60	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    61	// 3. DOMAIN ERRORS
    62	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    63	
    64	type ExecutorConfigIssue = {
    65	  path: PropertyKey[];
    66	  message: string;
    67	};
    68	
    69	/** Error thrown when a reserved executor kind is selected before wiring exists. */
    70	export class ExecutorNotWiredError extends Error {
    71	  kind: Extract<ExecutorKind, 'cli-claude-code'>;
    72	
    73	  constructor(kind: Extract<ExecutorKind, 'cli-claude-code'>) {
    74	    super(`Executor kind '${kind}' is reserved in the schema but not yet wired. Awaiting future spec for ${kind} integration.`);
    75	    this.name = 'ExecutorNotWiredError';
    76	    this.kind = kind;
    77	  }
    78	}
    79	
    80	/** Error thrown when executor configuration validation fails. */
    81	export class ExecutorConfigError extends Error {
    82	  issues: ExecutorConfigIssue[];
    83	
    84	  constructor(input: { issues: ExecutorConfigIssue[] }) {
    85	    super(input.issues.map((issue) => `${issue.path.map(String).join('.') || '<root>'}: ${issue.message}`).join('; '));
    86	    this.name = 'ExecutorConfigError';
    87	    this.issues = input.issues;
    88	  }
    89	}
    90	
    91	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    92	// 4. HELPERS
    93	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    94	
    95	function isRecord(value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> {
    96	  return value !== null && !Array.isArray(value) && typeof value === 'object';
    97	}
    98	
    99	function normalizeSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): SandboxMode {
   100	  return mode ?? 'workspace-write';
   101	}
   102	
   103	function normalizeExecutorConfigInput(raw: unknown): unknown {
   104	  if (!isRecord(raw) || !Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(raw, 'type')) {
   105	    return raw;
   106	  }
   107	
   108	  const legacyKind = raw.type;
   109	  if (typeof legacyKind !== 'string') {
   110	    return raw;
   111	  }
   112	
   113	  if (typeof raw.kind === 'string' && raw.kind !== legacyKind) {
   114	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   115	      issues: [{
   116	        path: ['type'],
   117	        message: `deprecated executor field 'type' conflicts with canonical kind '${raw.kind}'`,
   118	      }],
   119	    });
   120	  }
   121	
   122	  console.warn("[executor-config] Deprecated executor field 'type' was provided; use 'kind' instead.");
   123	  const { type: _legacyType, ...rest } = raw;
   124	  return {
   125	    ...rest,
   126	    kind: typeof raw.kind === 'string' ? raw.kind : legacyKind,
   127	  };
   128	}
   129	
   130	function normalizeIssues(error: z.ZodError<ExecutorConfig>): ExecutorConfigIssue[] {
   240	// the loop runs N executor "lineages" concurrently (capped), each running the
   241	// existing sequential loop in its own isolated sub-packet. The single-executor
   242	// path (executorConfigSchema / parseExecutorConfig) is untouched and remains the
   243	// default; callers use EITHER a single `executor` OR a `fanout` block, never both.
   244	
   245	const LINEAGE_LABEL_PATTERN = /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/;
   246	
   247	/**
   248	 * One fan-out lineage: a single executor config plus fan-out metadata.
   249	 *
   250	 * - `label`: directory-safe id for the lineage's isolated sub-packet.
   251	 * - `count`: number of replicas of THIS config to run (e.g. 5x the same model).
   252	 * - `iterations`: per-lineage max-iterations override; null = packet default.
   253	 * - `promptFramework`: optional per-model prompt framing key (e.g. "costar",
   254	 *   "tidd-ec") resolved against sk-prompt/prompt-models when a consumer renders the
   255	 *   lineage prompt. null = consumer default. Loop-type-agnostic and optional, so
   256	 *   research/review lineages that omit it are unaffected.
   257	 * - `assignment_model`: defaults to the existing flat pool; `wave` is accepted
   258	 *   only so the runtime guard can reject activation attempts explicitly.
   259	 * - `depends_on` / `touches`: reserved dependency and path-domain metadata for
   260	 *   a future guarded planner; flat-pool execution ignores them after logging.
   261	 */
   262	export const lineageExecutorSchema = executorConfigSchema.extend({
   263	  label: z.string().min(1).regex(LINEAGE_LABEL_PATTERN, {
   264	    message: "label must match /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/ (lowercase, digits, hyphens; dir-safe)",
   265	  }),
   266	  count: z.number().int().positive().max(16).default(1),
   267	  iterations: z.number().int().positive().nullable().default(null),
   268	  promptFramework: z.string().min(1).nullable().default(null),
   269	  assignment_model: z.enum(FANOUT_ASSIGNMENT_MODELS).default('flat_pool'),
   270	  depends_on: z.array(z.string().trim().min(1)).default([]),
   271	  touches: z.array(z.string().trim().min(1)).default([]),
   272	});
   273	
   274	export type LineageExecutor = z.infer<typeof lineageExecutorSchema>;
   275	
   276	export const fanoutConfigSchema = z.object({
   277	  // Ceiling of 16 lineages per fan-out block; combined with the per-lineage `count`
   278	  // ceiling (also 16) this bounds the maximum expanded lineage count a single
   279	  // autonomous run can reach.
   280	  executors: z.array(lineageExecutorSchema).min(1).max(16),
   281	  assignment_model: z.enum(FANOUT_ASSIGNMENT_MODELS).default('flat_pool'),
   282	  concurrency: z.number().int().positive().max(8).default(2),
   283	  maxRetries: z.number().int().nonnegative().max(5).default(5),
   284	  // Stall detection defaults ON and is non-disableable while running autonomously: a
   285	  // lineage that stops emitting progress is aborted and requeued within the ceiling and
   286	  // fails loud, instead of hanging silently at 0% CPU until the hours-scale subprocess
   287	  // timeout. Capped at 5 minutes; zero (the old opt-out) is now rejected, not accepted.
   288	  lagCeilingMs: z.number().int().positive().max(300000).default(300000),
   289	  progressHeartbeatSeconds: z.number().nonnegative().default(60),
   290	});
   291	
   292	export type FanoutConfig = z.infer<typeof fanoutConfigSchema>;
   293	
   294	/**
   295	 * Parse and validate a raw fan-out configuration.
   296	 *
   297	 * Each entry's executor subset is routed through {@link parseExecutorConfig} so
   298	 * ALL existing kind/model/flag rules apply per lineage with zero duplication.
   299	 * Labels must be unique and base-label (count>1) expansion must not collide.
   300	 *
   301	 * @param raw - Raw input to parse (JSON-parsed object).
   302	 * @returns Validated FanoutConfig.
   303	 * @throws {@link ExecutorConfigError} If validation fails.
   304	 */
   305	export function parseFanoutConfig(raw: unknown): FanoutConfig {
   306	  const parsed = fanoutConfigSchema.safeParse(raw);
   307	  if (!parsed.success) {
   308	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({ issues: parsed.error.issues.map((issue) => ({ path: issue.path, message: issue.message })) });
   309	  }
   310	
   311	  const config = parsed.data;
   312	
   313	  // Reuse the canonical single-executor validator per entry (kind/model/flags).
   314	  config.executors.forEach((entry, index) => {
   315	    const {
   316	      label: _label,
   317	      count: _count,
   318	      iterations: _iterations,
   319	      promptFramework: _promptFramework,
   320	      assignment_model: _assignmentModel,
   321	      depends_on: _dependsOn,
   322	      touches: _touches,
   323	      ...executorSubset
   324	    } = entry;
   325	    try {
   326	      parseExecutorConfig(executorSubset);
   327	    } catch (err: unknown) {
   328	      if (err instanceof ExecutorConfigError) {
   329	        throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   330	          issues: err.issues.map((issue) => ({ path: ['executors', index, ...issue.path], message: issue.message })),
   331	        });
   332	      }
   333	      throw err;
   334	    }
   335	  });
   336	
   337	  // Labels must be unique among the declared lineages.
   338	  const seenLabels = new Set<string>();
   339	  config.executors.forEach((entry, index) => {
   340	    if (seenLabels.has(entry.label)) {
   341	      throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   342	        issues: [{ path: ['executors', index, 'label'], message: `duplicate lineage label '${entry.label}'` }],
   343	      });
   344	    }
   345	    seenLabels.add(entry.label);
   346	  });
   347	
   348	  // Expanded labels (count>1 → label-1, label-2, …) must not collide either.
   349	  const expandedLabels = new Set<string>();
   350	  for (const lineage of expandLineages(config)) {
   351	    if (expandedLabels.has(lineage.label)) {
   352	      throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   353	        issues: [{ path: ['executors'], message: `expanded lineage label '${lineage.label}' collides; rename base labels` }],
   354	      });
   355	    }
   356	    expandedLabels.add(lineage.label);
   357	  }
   358	
   359	  return config;
   360	}
   361	
   362	/**
   363	 * Expand a fan-out config into concrete per-replica lineages.
   364	 *
   365	 * A `count` of 1 keeps the base label; `count > 1` yields `${label}-1` …
   366	 * `${label}-N`. Each expanded lineage carries `count: 1` so downstream
   367	 * consumers see one runnable unit per element.
   368	 *
   369	 * @param config - Validated FanoutConfig.
   370	 * @returns Flat list of single-replica lineages (one runnable unit each).
   371	 */
   372	export function expandLineages(config: FanoutConfig): LineageExecutor[] {
   373	  const lineages: LineageExecutor[] = [];
   374	  for (const entry of config.executors) {
   375	    if (entry.count === 1) {
   376	      lineages.push({ ...entry, count: 1 });
   377	      continue;
   378	    }
   379	    for (let replica = 1; replica <= entry.count; replica += 1) {
   380	      lineages.push({ ...entry, label: `${entry.label}-${replica}`, count: 1 });
   381	    }
   382	  }
   383	  return lineages;
   384	}

codex
The code already guarantees one useful kind of determinism: pool results are stored by declared input index and merge records are content-sorted. The gaps are elsewhere: replica identity is label/ordinal-based, retries are keyed only by label, all branches are launched up front, and `fanout-merge` reports `status: ok` even when lineages are absent or registry-less. I’m testing external designs specifically against those four facts.

codex
A stronger analogue emerged than generic quorum systems: indexed Kubernetes Jobs couple stable branch indices with success policies that can terminate remaining work once a subset succeeds. That gives R4 a concrete separation between “enough valid branches to decide” and “all scheduled branches completed,” while Volcano adds the complementary staged search pattern: stop after a satisfactory gradient, widen only when the current gradient cannot satisfy readiness.

.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-review/SKILL.md:55:**COMMAND-DRIVEN FAN-OUT IS SUPPORTED:** use `--executor`/`--executors`/`--concurrency` flags on `/deep:review`. The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-review/SKILL.md:354:**VERDICT_LOCK:** Any confirmed active P0 forces the exact final line `Review verdict: FAIL` -- never relabel that state as conditional, partial, mixed, or advisory, and truncated/partial output is not a valid substitute for the final line. An optional advisory `riskScore` may appear in narratives/JSONL for relative risk calibration but never changes the `PASS|CONDITIONAL|FAIL` mapping.

.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/council_graph_integration/council_graph_query_five_modes_prompt_safe_context.md:19:The query surface is the council synthesis caller's primary handle into derived graph state. .opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_cli_lineages_research.md:3:description: "Validate that a two-CLI-lineage fan-out research config triggers step_fanout_spawn → fanout-run.cjs pool → two isolated lineages sub-packets → step_fanout_merge → canonical research.md."
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_cli_lineages_research.md:27:- Objective: Confirm the research YAML dispatches CLI lineages via `fanout-run.cjs` when two executor flags are present, that both lineages are isolated, and that the merged registry feeds `step_compile_research`.
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_cli_lineages_research.md:29:- Expected execution process: 1. Setup binds two executors → `config.fanout` (not `config.executor`). 2. `step_resolve_artifact_root` resolves base `artifact_dir`. 3. `step_fanout_spawn_cli` calls `fanout-run.cjs` with both CLI lineages. 4. Pool spawns two subprocesses in parallel. 5. Each writes to `lineages/opencode/` and `lineages/claude/`. 6. `step_fanout_merge` reads both lineage registries, deduplicates. 7. `step_compile_research` emits canonical `research.md`.
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_cli_lineages_research.md:30:- Expected signals: `step_fanout_spawn` present in YAML with `skip_when`; `step_fanout_merge` present in `phase_synthesis` with `skip_when`; `--executor` docs list 2+ → `config.fanout` default policy; `fanout-run.vitest.ts` confirms pool isolation.
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_cli_lineages_research.md:41:- `fanout-run.cjs` and `fanout-merge.cjs` present.

.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-ai-council/README.md:112:Convergence is not a popularity vote. A plan converges when two of three seats agree on the material direction and the critique pass finds no new high-severity blocker. The shipped config also declares the shared anti-convergence floor for council mode: `antiConvergence.minRounds = 2`, `convergenceMode = "default"` and `stopPolicy = "fail-closed"`. If the council hits `max_rounds` without hitting that bar, the run emits `non-converged`. Preserved failed-round forensics let you inspect what blocked convergence and decide whether to add a round, change lenses or accept the partial result.
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-ai-council/README.md:153:| Council emits `non-converged` | The council hit `max_rounds` without two-of-three agreement and refused to fake consensus | Read the failed-round forensics under `failed/round-NNN-<timestamp>/`. Add a round with different lenses or accept the partial result as the honest answer. |
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-ai-council/README.md:186:A: The run completes as `non-converged`. This is an honest answer: the evidence did not settle toward one plan. .opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-research/README.md:179:Deterministic scenarios under `manual_testing_playbook/` cover loop lifecycle, state management, convergence and recovery, and research output. Preconditions, expected signals and pass, fail or partial verdict rules are defined in the root playbook. Every scenario maps to a dedicated feature file with the canonical prompt, expected signals and live source anchors.
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-review/feature_catalog/loop_lifecycle/resource_map_emission.md:29:The emitter is enabled by default through `config.resource_map.emit = true`. .opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_cli_lineages_review.md:3:description: "Validate that a two-CLI-lineage fan-out review config triggers step_fanout_spawn → fanout-run.cjs pool → two isolated lineages sub-packets → step_fanout_merge with p0/p1/p2 binding → step_derive_verdict using merged counts → review-report.md."
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_cli_lineages_review.md:31:- Expected execution process: 1. Setup binds two executors → `config.fanout`. 2. `step_fanout_spawn_cli` calls `fanout-run.cjs --loop-type review`. 3. Pool spawns two subprocesses. 4. `step_fanout_merge` calls `fanout-merge.cjs --loop-type review` and binds `active_p0/p1/p2` → `p0_count/p1_count/p2_count`. 5. `step_derive_verdict` derives verdict from bound counts.

.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-research/changelog/v1.10.0.0.md:61:Phase 017's `generate-description.js` collapsed parse failures and schema violations into the same `null` branch, which then rebuilt a minimal replacement that dropped authored rich content. The 30-iteration research quantified the blast radius: 29 of 86 packet-level `description.json` files in the `026` tree were one invalid edit away from lossy repair. The loader now returns a discriminated result `{ok, reason: 'file_missing' | 'parse_error' | 'schema_error', partial?}`. The new `mergePreserveRepair` helper preserves authored narrative plus extension keys when the file is parseable but schema-invalid. Canonical derived fields always win on conflict. A new specimen regression suite uses real rich 017 packet files. The `SPECKIT_DESCRIPTION_REPAIR_MERGE_SAFE` feature flag defaults to on. Setting it to `false` reverts to Phase 017 behavior for rollback.
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_strongest_restriction.md:30:- Expected signals: `SEVERITY_RANK` map (`{P0:3, P1:2, P2:1}`) in `fanout-merge.cjs`; `status !== 'active'` guard; `mergedVerdict=FAIL` when `activeP0>0`; `mergedVerdict=CONDITIONAL` when `activeP1>0` only; `mergedVerdict=PASS` when all empty; duplicate `findingId` with P2+P0 escalates to P0 and produces single finding.
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/fanout/fanout_strongest_restriction.md:40:- `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` present.

.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/convergence_and_recovery/fail_closed_reducer.md:19:Fail-closed behavior protects review state from quietly drifting into partial writes or ambiguous recovery. Operators need a predictable contract: corruption must stop the reducer by default, missing anchors must block unsafe rewrites by default, and both escape hatches must be explicit so recovery stays intentional.

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/compiled/deep_review.contract.md:229:**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Review fan-out uses strongest-restriction: any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL. Native and CLI fan-out lineages run under `fanout-run.cjs` in the capped pool so every lineage shares retries, artifact checks, and stop-policy validation. The native adapter calls the `deep/review` command with the internal `--fanout-lineage-artifact-dir` override; it must not dispatch the LEAF `@deep-review` agent or a pasted phase-running prompt as a full-loop sub-agent. When fan-out includes `cli-opencode`, that lineage is an explicit parallel-detached OpenCode session with its own lineage directory and session id; this is the documented exception to the cli-opencode self-invocation guard. Single-executor `cli-opencode` remains cross-runtime only.
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-ai-council/references/patterns/anti_patterns.md:54:| **Ignoring Low Scorers** | Low-scoring seats may have valuable partial insights | Score everything, cherry-pick good elements |
.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-ai-council/references/patterns/command_wiring.md:160:The helper exits `1` when required report sections are missing. It exits `2` when artifact or payload writes partially fail. Callers should surface that status and avoid running `generate-context.js` if the payload file was not written.

 succeeded in 0ms:
   140	      command: "node -e \"const { resolveArtifactRoot } = require('.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/shared/review-research-paths.cjs'); console.log(JSON.stringify(resolveArtifactRoot('{spec_folder}', 'research')));\""
   141	      outputs:
   142	        - artifact_root_dir: "Resolved local research owner directory"
   143	        - artifact_subfolder: "Local packet subfolder ({ownerSlug}-pt-{NN}), else null"
   144	        - artifact_dir: "Resolved research packet directory for this target"
   145	        - artifact_archive_root: "Resolved local research archive directory for this target"
   146	
   147	  step_fanout_spawn:
   148	    description: "Spawn fan-out lineages; skip and proceed to phase_init normally when single-executor"
   149	    skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
   150	    instructions: |
   151	      Fan-out mode is active. Spawn all lineages. Each lineage runs the full loop in its own
   152	      isolated sub-packet under {artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/.
   153	      fanout-run.cjs owns native and CLI lineage execution; native is run through the
   154	      deep/research command surface with config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir so the LEAF
   155	      deep-research agent is used only for individual iterations, never as a full-loop
   156	      sub-agent or pasted phase-running prompt.
   157	      After all lineages complete, jump directly to phase_synthesis — skip phase_init and
   158	      phase_main_loop entirely (those are the single-executor path). The merge step in
   159	      phase_synthesis will consolidate results across all lineages.
   160	    sub_steps:
   161	      step_fanout_spawn_cli:
   162	        description: "Spawn all native and CLI lineages in capped worker pool"
   163	        skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors"
   164	        command: |
   165	          node .opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs \
   166	            --spec-folder {spec_folder} \
   167	            --loop-type research \
   168	            --research-topic "{research_topic}" \
   169	            --fanout-config-json '{config.fanout_json}' \
   170	            --base-artifact-dir {artifact_dir} \
   171	            --convergence-threshold {convergence_threshold}
   172	        working_directory: "{repo_root}"
   173	        wait_for_completion: true
   174	
   175	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   176	  # PHASE 1: INITIALIZATION
   177	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   178	  phase_init:
   179	    purpose: Create all state files for the research session
   180	    steps:
   181	
   182	      step_preflight_contract:
   183	        action: "Validate required setup bindings and executable workflow contract before any file writes"
   184	        verify:
   185	          - required_values_present: [research_topic, spec_folder, max_iterations, convergence_threshold, convergence_mode]
   186	          - spec_folder_is_within: "specs/ or .opencode/specs/ (canonicalize alias roots before validation)"

context compacted
codex
R4 needs a persisted fan-in decision machine between pool execution and merge. The current runtime has deterministic output ordering, but not deterministic branch identity, selection, or partial-failure semantics.

The local evidence:

- Branches are expanded from mutable labels and replica ordinals in [executor-config.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts:372).
- The pool queues every input immediately and retries by label/index in [fanout-pool.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/scripts/fanout-pool.cjs:418).
- `fanout-run` reserves the aggregate worst-case budget before launching all lineages in [fanout-run.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs:1535).
- Merge sorts content deterministically, but filters missing registries and still returns `status: "ok"` in [fanout-merge.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs:921).
- The auto workflow explicitly spawns all lineages before synthesis in [deep_research_auto.yaml](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_research_auto.yaml:147).

That combination makes presentation deterministic after the fact. It does not ensure the same logical branches are selected when completion order, retries, labels, or failures change.

### Proposed runtime contract

Add a policy evaluator returning one of four persisted transitions:

```text
WAIT    insufficient terminal evidence
ACCEPT  freeze a qualified branch set and permit merge
WIDEN   reserve budget and enqueue the next canonical stage
BLOCK   policy cannot be satisfied
```

Each decision should record `policy_version`, JSONL offset, stage, terminal branch states, selected logical branch IDs, missing required evidence, budget snapshot, deadline, and reason. Merge should require an `ACCEPT` event; it should never independently decide whether missing branches are tolerable.

Use explicit immutable `branch_key` values from configuration. Derive `logical_branch_id` from policy version, stage ID, and branch key; represent retries separately as `logical_branch_id/attempt-N`. Labels remain display metadata. Taskflow’s condition-task API demonstrates why ordinal successor indexes are attractive but fragile: their meaning depends on construction order. Its scheduler documentation also warns that multiple conditional paths can schedule the same task, implying the need for a branch lease or deduplication key. [Taskflow conditional tasking](https://taskflow.github.io/taskflow/ConditionalTasking.html)

Determinism also requires barriered decisions. “Accept the first three successes” remains timing-dependent even with stable IDs. Evaluate final acceptance only at explicit barriers: all branches in the current stage terminal, persisted deadline reached, budget exhaustion, or required-evidence impossibility. Progressive mode may publish provisional revisions after individual completions, but its final accepted projection still needs a barrier.

### Conditional fan-in

Model stages similarly to Volcano’s topology gradients:

1. Run a small, deliberately diverse seed stage.
2. At its barrier, apply cheap deterministic checks: normalized agreement, critical contradictions, evidence coverage, execution validity, and required-role presence.
3. `ACCEPT` on strong agreement plus quality coverage.
4. `WIDEN` on disagreement or deficient coverage when the next stage has adequate expected value and budget can be reserved atomically.
5. `BLOCK` or policy-authorized partial acceptance when no widening path remains.

Volcano’s scheduler tests progressively broader candidate gradients and skips later gradients once readiness is satisfied. Its gang scheduler defines readiness through `MinAvailable`, rather than waiting for every task. [Volcano design](https://github.com/volcano-sh/volcano/blob/master/docs/design/Network%20Topology%20Aware%20Scheduling.md), [gang scheduler](https://github.com/volcano-sh/volcano/blob/master/pkg/scheduler/plugins/gang/gang.go)

RouteLLM supplies a useful widening primitive: compare a calibrated quality estimate with a threshold and fail safe to the stronger route when the estimate is invalid. For deep-loop, the score should use observed seed-stage disagreement and evidence coverage—not merely the original prompt—and estimate marginal quality gain per reserved token. [RouteLLM router](https://github.com/lm-sys/RouteLLM/blob/main/routellm/routers/routers.py), [paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.18665)

### Partial-failure semantics

The four policies should have materially different contracts:

| Policy | Acceptance rule |
|---|---|
| `strict` | Every required logical branch finishes validly; any required failure blocks. |
| `quorum` | A qualified count succeeds, required roles/evidence are present, and no veto-class result is unresolved. Selection uses canonical priority/ID order, never arrival order. |
| `deadline` | At a persisted deadline, freeze eligible receipts. Missing required evidence blocks or widens according to policy; later completions are recorded but cannot mutate the accepted projection. |
| `progressive` | Emit versioned provisional reductions, then finalize only at a deterministic barrier. |

Kubernetes Indexed Jobs are the strongest reference implementation here: success rules can require particular indexes, a count, or a count within a specified subset; rules are evaluated in order and remaining Pods are terminated after a match. Failure rules are also ordered, and failure criteria take precedence over simultaneous success. [Kubernetes Job documentation](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/), [success-policy reducer](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/pkg/controller/job/success_policy.go)

Tekton shows that failure tolerance must be consumer-sensitive. `onError: continue` can preserve usable results, but a downstream task that consumes a missing result still fails. Deep-loop should therefore classify branch evidence as `required`, `optional`, or `advisory` for the chosen reducer, rather than treating partial failure as one run-level boolean. [Tekton task failure handling](https://tekton.dev/docs/pipelines/tasks/), [result-reference resolution](https://github.com/tektoncd/pipeline/blob/main/pkg/reconciler/pipelinerun/resources/resultrefresolution.go)

Finally, cancellation is not acceptance. AWS Distributed Map documents that child executions can continue briefly after a tolerated-failure threshold is exceeded. Deep-loop should append separate decision, cancellation-request, cancellation-confirmed, and late-result events; late results belong to observability, not the frozen merge projection. [AWS Distributed Map failure thresholds](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/step-functions/latest/dg/state-map-distributed.html)
